desc_en: The NCEDC Calpine/Unocal Geysers dataset is a historical microearthquake waveform archive for The Geysers geothermal field in California, derived from borehole and surface monitoring operated by Unocal and later Calpine. NCEDC states that the network included up to 49 borehole monitoring sites and released digital microearthquake seismograms for 1989-2000; the assembled directory provides annual tgz waveform archives and an unedited 1995-1998 event catalog used mainly as an index to the waveforms. It is distinct from the already cataloged GEISER 2012-2013 broadband dataset and is a valuable access route for long-term geothermal induced microseismicity studies.
openness: public NCEDC distribution with license unspecified
source_type: seismic waveform archive and event catalog
evidence: NCEDC states that Calpine continued Unocal's collaboration with NCEDC and made the data available for public distribution; it describes the dataset as digital microearthquake seismograms recorded by the Calpine/Unocal Geysers network, with more than 10 years of seismograms released for archiving and distribution. The data-access section says the 1989-2000 microearthquake waveform data are available as an assembled dataset, and the directory lists annual geysers.1989.tgz through geysers.2000.tgz files. The direct catalog text file describes a 1995-1998 unedited earthquake catalog with approximate times and rough hypocenters.
scale: field-scale historical geothermal microearthquake waveform archive
feature_count: up to 49 borehole monitoring sites; annual waveform archives for 1989-2000; 1995-1998 event index catalog
file_size: annual waveform tgz files listed from 442 MB to 2.1 GB; catalog.txt content-length 5,106,310 bytes

name: NCEDC Geysers CEC dense temporary seismic network archive
provider: Northern California Earthquake Data Center / California Energy Commission Geysers project / UC Berkeley
theme_raw: Temporary California Energy Commission dense seismic network at The Geysers geothermal reservoir
coverage: The Geysers geothermal reservoir, California, United States
license: public NCEDC distribution; explicit open-data license not stated on inspected pages
formats: miniSEED data per metadata document, raw station swap files, DOCX metadata, XLSX station coordinates, web directory indexes
access: NCEDC assembled archive directory with year folders, raw swap folders, metadata document and station-coordinate spreadsheet
doi_or_citation: NCEDC assembled dataset directory; Monitoring reservoir processes at The Geysers geothermal field, Geothermal Energy, 2026, https://doi.org/10.1186/s40517-026-00384-7
time_span: network deployment June 2018 through May 2024; directory also lists 2018-2025 folders and raw swap folders
notes: Distinct from the v013 Calpine/Unocal 1989-2000 archive and the v003 GEISER 2012-2013 broadband row. The CEC archive represents a dense temporary array used for reservoir monitoring and passive seismic imaging at The Geysers. Local inspection of the NCEDC metadata document reports 91 stations, a 5 km by 5 km array, about 500 m station spacing, 3-component 4.5 Hz geophones, 24-bit conversion, 200 samples per second, 20 TB data volume and miniSEED format. The raw directory exposes Swap1 through Swap17 folders with multi-GB RAW files.
desc_zh: NCEDC 的 Geysers CEC 归档收录加州 The Geysers 地热储层临时 California Energy Commission 密集地震台阵数据。元数据说明该网络 2018 年 6 月至 2024 年 5 月部署，共 91 个三分量 4.5 Hz 地震检波器台站，覆盖约 5 km x 5 km 区域，台站间距约 500 m，200 sps 采样，总数据量约 20 TB，格式为 miniSEED。该数据与 Calpine/Unocal 1989-2000 历史微震归档和 GEISER 2012-2013 宽频数据不同，是面向 The Geysers 地热储层现代高密度被动地震监测和诱发微震成像的重要开放入口。
desc_en: The NCEDC Geysers CEC archive provides data from a temporary California Energy Commission dense seismic network at The Geysers geothermal reservoir in California. The metadata document states that the deployment ran from June 2018 through May 2024 with 91 three-component 4.5 Hz geophone stations over an approximately 5 km by 5 km array, about 500 m station spacing, 200 samples per second and roughly 20 TB of miniSEED data. It is distinct from the Calpine/Unocal 1989-2000 historical archive and the GEISER 2012-2013 broadband row, and is a high-value open route for modern geothermal induced microseismicity monitoring and passive seismic imaging at The Geysers.
openness: public NCEDC distribution with license unspecified
source_type: seismic waveform archive and station metadata
evidence: The NCEDC directory exposes 2018-2025 year folders, a raw folder, CEC_Meta_Data.docx and Station_Coordinates.xlsx. Local extraction of the metadata document reports June 2018-May 2024 deployment, 91 stations, 5 km x 5 km geometry, 500 m station spacing, 3-component 4.5 Hz geophones, 24-bit conversion, 200 sps, 20 TB data volume and miniSEED format. The station spreadsheet has 92 rows including the header, i.e. 91 station-coordinate records. The raw/Swap1 directory lists many multi-GB RAW files. A 2026 Geothermal Energy paper states that passive seismic data were collected and processed from the temporary CEC seismic network at The Geysers and the permanent Berkeley Geysers network.
scale: dense temporary geothermal reservoir seismic array
feature_count: 91 stations; 17 raw swap folders observed; metadata reports 10,000 to 30,000 detectable/extractable events per year depending on picker
file_size: metadata reports 20 TB total data volume; observed raw files include multi-GB RAW files; metadata docx 15,397 bytes and station-coordinate xlsx 12,059 bytes

name: Synthetic cases of injection induced seismicity for DEEP forecasting models
provider: Zenodo / DEEP - Geothermica / ETH Zurich
theme_raw: TOUGH2-SEED synthetic induced-seismicity catalogues and hydraulic injection time series
coverage: synthetic geothermal/EGS injection scenarios; no-fault and fault-domain cases
formats: ZIP archive, MATLAB .mat seismic catalogues, CSV hydraulic time series, README markdown
access: Zenodo landing page, Zenodo API record, direct README and Dataset.zip downloads
doi_or_citation: Ritz (2024), https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.11490087; Mignan, Lanza and Rinaldi (2023) DEEP Deliverable 3.2; Rinaldi and Nespoli (2017), https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cageo.2016.12.003
notes: Accepted as a synthetic benchmark rather than observed field seismicity. The README states that the synthetic cases were created with TOUGH2-SEED for DEEP Deliverable 3.2, based on Ritz et al. (2022). Cases include no-fault and fault domains, fixed and variable permeability, fixed and variable b-value behavior, and step/linear/constant/cyclic injection strategies. Each case includes hydraulic data in Injector_time_series.csv and seismic catalogues for 48 stochastic realisations in MATLAB files.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布 DEEP 项目用于诱发地震预测模型的合成注入诱发地震案例，由 TOUGH2-SEED 生成。README 说明数据包含无断层和有断层情景、固定/可变渗透率、固定/可变 b 值以及阶跃、线性、恒定和周期性注入策略；每个案例包含注入点压力、温度、气体饱和度、注入速率等 hydraulic time series，以及 48 个随机实现的地震目录。它不是实测地震台网数据，但适合用于人工/诱发地震建模、预测和机器学习方法测试。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases synthetic injection-induced seismicity cases for induced-seismicity forecasting models in the DEEP project, generated with TOUGH2-SEED. The README describes no-fault and fault-domain scenarios, fixed and variable permeability, fixed and variable b-value behavior, and step, linear, constant and cyclic injection strategies; each case includes injector hydraulic time series and seismic catalogues for 48 stochastic realisations. It is not observed field-network data, but it is a useful open benchmark for modelling, forecasting and machine-learning studies of anthropogenic/injection-induced seismicity.
openness: open repository record with CC-BY-4.0 license
evidence: Zenodo record lists Dataset/Open status, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.11490087, CC-BY-4.0 license, Dataset.zip (297,265,610 bytes) and README.md (6,666 bytes). The README documents TOUGH2-SEED generation, injection scenarios, hydraulic data columns and MATLAB seismic catalogues with 21 columns including coordinates, time, magnitude, stresses, stress drop, b-value and triggering indicators.
feature_count: 48 stochastic realisations per case; seismic catalogues with 21 documented columns

name: San Emidio 2022 microseismic event catalog and 3-D P-wave velocity model
provider: Zenodo / University of Wisconsin-Madison / WHOLESCALE project
theme_raw: San Emidio geothermal-field microseismic event catalog and tomographic Vp model
coverage: San Emidio geothermal field, Nevada, United States
license: Creative Commons Attribution Non Commercial No Derivatives 4.0 International
formats: CSV event catalog and CSV 3-D P-wave velocity model
access: Zenodo landing page, API record and direct CSV downloads
doi_or_citation: Guo et al. (2025), https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14695234; related manuscript Microseismicity Modulation Due to Changes in Geothermal Production at San Emidio, Nevada, USA
notes: Accepted as a derived model/data companion rather than a duplicate of the existing GDR San Emidio event-catalog row. The existing v006 GDR row covers a high-precision microseismic event catalog from GDR submission 1614, while this Zenodo record additionally provides a 3-D tomographic P-wave velocity model with resolvability values. The Zenodo event catalog has 1,761 data rows and the Vp model has 3,600 data rows in local CSV inspection.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布 Nevada San Emidio 地热场 2022 年 WHOLESCALE 项目的微震事件目录及 3D P 波速度模型。事件目录包含发震时刻、震级、WGS84 经纬度、UTM 坐标、高程和位置不确定度；Vp 模型包含纬度、经度、UTM 坐标、高程、P 波速度和模型可分辨度。它与已收录的 GDR San Emidio 事件目录相关，但额外提供了用于解释地热生产变化与微震调制的三维速度结构派生数据。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases a San Emidio 2022 WHOLESCALE microseismic event catalog together with a 3-D P-wave velocity model for the Nevada geothermal field. The event catalog includes origin time, magnitude, WGS84 coordinates, UTM coordinates, elevation and location uncertainties, while the Vp model provides latitude, longitude, UTM coordinates, elevation, P-wave velocity and resolvability. It is related to the existing GDR San Emidio event-catalog row but adds a distinct tomographic velocity-model product for interpreting microseismicity modulation during geothermal production.
source_type: data repository derived seismic model and event catalog
evidence: Zenodo record lists Dataset/Open status, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14695234, CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0 license, SanEmidio_2022MicroseismicEventCatalog.csv (173,998 bytes) and SanEmidio_2022Vpmodel.csv (209,216 bytes). Local CSV inspection found 1,761 event rows with origin time, magnitude, relocated coordinates and uncertainty fields, and 3,600 Vp-model rows with coordinates, Vp(m/s) and resolvability.
scale: site-specific derived microseismic catalog and 3-D velocity model
feature_count: 1,761 event rows; 3,600 Vp model rows
file_size: event catalog CSV 173,998 bytes; Vp model CSV 209,216 bytes

name: Phase picks for Raton Basin earthquakes 2016-2020
provider: Zenodo / University of New Mexico / Dalhousie University / New Mexico Tech / University of Arizona
theme_raw: Raton Basin broadband and nodal-geophone earthquake phase-arrival dataset
coverage: Raton Basin, Colorado-New Mexico border, United States
formats: TXT origins files, TXT phase-arrival files, station list, README
access: Zenodo landing page, API record and direct text-file downloads
doi_or_citation: Glasgow et al. (2021), https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.5579804; related paper Glasgow et al. (2021), Raton Basin induced seismicity is hosted by networks of short basement faults and mimics tectonic earthquake statistics, https://doi.org/10.1029/2021JB022839
notes: Distinct from the existing ISC Raton Basin 2018 Nodal Array Catalog row, which represents a one-month dense nodal catalog. This Zenodo record provides phase arrival information for both broadband seismometers and nodal geophones from 2016 to 2020, including origins and phases for broadband-only and nodal-plus-broadband datasets. The README states the files were built with PhaseNet, REAL and VELEST.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布 Raton Basin 2016-2020 年地震的相位到时数据，覆盖 Colorado-New Mexico 边界附近长期废水注入相关诱发地震区。数据包含宽频台站和节点地震仪的事件震源文件、P/S 到时文件、台站表和 README，README 说明处理流程使用 PhaseNet、REAL 和 VELEST。它与已收录的 Raton Basin 2018 节点台阵目录不同，提供更长时间跨度和相位级别的机器学习拾取/定位输入数据。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases phase-arrival data for Raton Basin earthquakes from 2016 to 2020 in the wastewater-injection induced seismicity region along the Colorado-New Mexico border. It includes broadband and nodal-geophone origin files, P/S phase files, a station list and a README; the README states that the files were built using PhaseNet, REAL and VELEST. It is distinct from the already cataloged Raton Basin 2018 Nodal Array Catalog because it provides a longer time span and phase-level inputs for machine-learning-based picking and relocation workflows.
openness: open repository record with CC-BY-4.0 license
evidence: Zenodo record lists Dataset/Open status, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.5579804, CC-BY-4.0 license, origins_broadband.txt, origins_nodalbroadband.txt, phases_broadband.txt, phases_nodalbroadband.txt, station_list.txt and readme.txt. The README states that origins files contain origin time and location, phases files contain event id, station, phase arrival time, PhaseNet pick probability and P/S phase, and station_list contains station name, latitude and longitude. Local file inspection found 36,208 broadband origins, 2,882 nodal-plus-broadband origins and 104 station-list rows.
feature_count: 36,208 broadband origins; 2,882 nodal-plus-broadband origins; 104 station-list rows; phase files provided for broadband and nodal-plus-broadband datasets
file_size: Zenodo files total 17.4 MB; phases_broadband.txt 8,301,648 bytes; phases_nodalbroadband.txt 6,692,871 bytes; origins_broadband.txt 2,193,959 bytes; origins_nodalbroadband.txt 171,516 bytes; station_list.txt 2,687 bytes; readme.txt 1,479 bytes

name: Machine-learning-derived Raton Basin seismic catalog 2016-2024
provider: Zenodo / Jamalreyhani, Wang, Schmandt, Pena Castro and Glasgow
theme_raw: PhaseNet and REAL derived Raton Basin earthquake catalog for GRL 2025 study
coverage: Raton Basin, Colorado-New Mexico border, United States
license: open Zenodo repository access; explicit license not exposed in the inspected Zenodo API record
formats: PF-style plain-text catalog file
access: Zenodo landing page, API record and direct catalog file download
doi_or_citation: Jamalreyhani et al. (2025), https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.15751302; related article Evidence for Fluid Pressurization of Fault Zones and Persistent Sensitivity to Injection Rate Beneath the Raton Basin, https://doi.org/10.1029/2025GL114675
notes: Distinct from the v017 phase-pick dataset. The v017 row provides phase arrivals and origins for 2016-2020 broadband/nodal workflows, while this v018 row is a later machine-learning-derived earthquake catalog for 2016-2024 with 95,993 events and local magnitudes from -1.0 to 4.3. The Zenodo record does not expose an explicit license in the inspected API response, so reuse status is recorded cautiously.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布 Raton Basin 2016-2024 年机器学习地震目录，是 2025 年 GRL 论文关于流体压力与注入率敏感性的补充数据。记录说明作者使用 PhaseNet 对 8 个宽频台站的连续波形进行相位拾取，再用 REAL 关联 P/S 到时并估计初始震源位置，得到 95,993 个地震事件，地方震级范围为 -1.0 至 4.3。它与 v017 的相位到时数据不同，是更长时段的事件级目录产品。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases a machine-learning-derived Raton Basin seismic catalog for 2016-2024 as the data supplement to a 2025 Geophysical Research Letters study on fluid pressurization and sensitivity to injection rate. The record states that PhaseNet was applied to eight years of continuous waveform data from eight broadband stations, and REAL was used to associate P and S arrivals and determine preliminary locations, yielding 95,993 earthquakes with local magnitudes from -1.0 to 4.3. It is distinct from the v017 phase-pick dataset because it is a later, longer event-level catalog product.
openness: open repository access; license not specified in inspected metadata
evidence: Zenodo API record lists title, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.15751302, publication date 2025-06-27, and catalog_all_up_mag.pf (16,463,432 bytes). The description states the catalog contains 95,993 earthquakes with local magnitudes -1.0 to 4.3 from 2016-2024, generated using PhaseNet and REAL from eight broadband stations. Partial local file inspection confirmed PF-style event entries with name, time, latitude, longitude, magnitude and depth fields.
feature_count: 95,993 earthquakes reported by Zenodo record

name: Raton Basin 2016-2024 moment-magnitude catalog for injection-induced earthquakes
provider: Zenodo / Peña Castro, Schmandt, Glasgow, Jamalreyhani, Wang and Cochran
theme_raw: Moment magnitudes for a 31,581-event subset of the Raton Basin induced earthquake catalog
coverage: Raton Basin, Colorado-New Mexico border, United States
formats: CSV
access: Zenodo landing page, API record and direct CSV file download
doi_or_citation: Peña Castro et al. (2026), https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20099746; related Seismica article https://doi.org/10.26443/seismica.v5i1.1959; source locations from Jamalreyhani et al. (2025), https://doi.org/10.1029/2025GL114675
notes: Distinct from the v018 95,993-event machine-learning Raton Basin catalog. This v019 row is a derived source-parameter product that adds moment magnitudes for 31,581 wastewater-injection-associated earthquakes and is intended for frequency-magnitude scaling analysis. Local inspection of the downloadable CSV confirmed 31,581 rows with Mw, event ID, latitude, longitude, depth, origin time and local magnitude columns.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布 Raton Basin 2016-2024 年注水诱发地震的矩震级目录，是 Seismica 论文关于小震矩震级和频率-震级标度分析的数据产品。数据为 CSV 格式，记录说明包含 31,581 个事件的 Mw；本地抽查确认字段包括 Mw、事件编号、经纬度、深度、发震时刻和地方震级。它与 v018 的 95,993 个事件检测/定位目录不同，重点是可直接用于震级标度和源参数分析的派生震级目录。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases a moment-magnitude catalog for wastewater-injection-associated earthquakes in the Raton Basin during 2016-2024, supporting a Seismica study of small-earthquake moment magnitude and frequency-magnitude scaling. The record provides a CSV file with Mw values for 31,581 events; local inspection confirmed columns for Mw, event ID, latitude, longitude, depth, origin time and local magnitude. It is distinct from the v018 95,993-event detection/location catalog because this row captures a derived source-parameter product for magnitude scaling analysis.
source_type: data repository derived earthquake source-parameter catalog
evidence: Zenodo API record 20099746 lists DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20099746, publication date 2026-02-24, license cc-by-4.0 and zenodo_cat_Raton_Basin.csv (1,984,477 bytes). The record description says the repository contains moment magnitudes for 31,581 Raton Basin events with locations from Jamalreyhani et al. (2025). Local CSV inspection confirmed 31,581 data rows and columns Mw, evID, Latitude, Longitude, Depth, OT and local_mag.
feature_count: 31,581 earthquakes confirmed by local CSV row count

name: Microearthquake Studies at the Salton Sea Geothermal Field earthquake catalog
provider: Geothermal Data Repository / Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory / Templeton
theme_raw: Matched Field Processing and relocated microearthquake catalog for the Salton Sea Geothermal Field
coverage: Salton Sea Geothermal Field, Imperial Valley, California, United States
formats: TXT earthquake catalog
access: Geothermal Data Repository landing page, DOI landing route and direct catalog text download
doi_or_citation: Templeton, D. (2013). Microearthquake Studies at the Salton Sea Geothermal Field. Geothermal Data Repository, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory. https://doi.org/10.15121/1148809
time_span: 2008-01-03 to 2011-12-31 in downloaded catalog; GDR data record dated 2013-10-01 and last updated 2017-05-26
notes: The GDR record describes a DOE geothermal project adapting Matched Field Processing to detect and locate more small events than conventional earthquake-location workflows. The downloadable file is named Final_Catalog_MFP_Haukkson.txt on GDR and combines events from the MFP detection technique and the original relocated catalog. Local inspection confirmed an event table with date, time, latitude, longitude and depth columns.
desc_zh: 该 Geothermal Data Repository 数据集发布 Salton Sea Geothermal Field 的微地震目录，目标是通过更多、更小事件的检测定位来表征地热储层裂隙网络。页面说明项目将 Matched Field Processing 方法用于地热储层弹性波传播问题，并提供一个合并的地震目录文件。下载抽查显示目录包含 8,383 条事件记录，时间从 2008 年 1 月到 2011 年 12 月，字段包括日期、时间、经纬度和深度。
desc_en: This Geothermal Data Repository dataset provides a microearthquake catalog for the Salton Sea Geothermal Field, intended to support geothermal reservoir fracture characterization through improved event detection and location. The record describes adapting Matched Field Processing to the elastic propagation problem in geothermal reservoirs and provides a combined earthquake catalog file. Local inspection of the direct download found 8,383 event rows from January 2008 through December 2011 with date, time, latitude, longitude and depth fields.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: DOE geothermal data repository earthquake catalog
evidence: GDR submission 250 lists DOI 10.15121/1148809, public access, CC-BY-4.0 licensing, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory organization, author Dennise Templeton, and a downloadable Earthquake Catalog.txt resource of 409.48 kB. Direct download from https://gdr.openei.org/files/250/Final_Catalog_MFP_Haukkson.txt returned 419,305 bytes. Local text inspection counted 8,384 lines including the header and 8,383 event rows from 2008/01/03 to 2011/12/31 with Date, Time, Latitude, Longitude and Depth-km columns.
feature_count: 8,383 event rows confirmed by local text-file inspection
file_size: Final_Catalog_MFP_Haukkson.txt 419,305 bytes; GDR page reports 409.48 kB

name: St1 Deep Heat Espoo 2018 relocated earthquake source-parameter catalog
provider: GFZ Data Services / St1 Deep Heat collaborators
theme_canonical: Enhanced geothermal induced seismicity source-parameter catalog
theme_raw: Source parameters of relocated earthquakes recorded during hydraulic stimulation within the St1 Deep Heat project in Espoo, Finland
coverage: Otaniemi, Espoo/Helsinki, southern Finland
formats: CSV, TXT README, XML metadata
access: GFZ Data Services DOI landing page and direct datapub CSV/README downloads
doi_or_citation: Kwiatek et al. (2019), https://doi.org/10.5880/GFZ.4.2.2019.001; related Science Advances article https://doi.org/10.1126/sciadv.aav7224
time_span: 2018-06-04 to 2018-07-24 in inspected CSV; 49-day stimulation and 10 days after completion described by GFZ record
notes: Distinct from the v020 Helsinki 2018 high-resolution event catalog row. This GFZ record is a refined source-parameter product for 1,977 relocated earthquakes, including injection phase, origin time, coordinates, local and moment magnitudes, pick count and bootstrap relocation uncertainty fields.
desc_zh: 该 GFZ Data Services 数据集发布芬兰 Espoo/Otaniemi St1 Deep Heat 2018 年水力刺激期间地震的重定位源参数目录。README 说明 CSV 包含 1,977 个经双差方法重定位的地震事件，本地抽查确认字段包括事件 ID、注入阶段、UTC 发震时刻、坐标、高程、本地震级、矩震级、拾取数和误差椭球参数。它与已有 Helsinki 2018 总事件目录不同，是更窄但更适合源参数和位置不确定性分析的派生目录。
desc_en: This GFZ Data Services dataset releases a relocated source-parameter catalog for earthquakes recorded during the 2018 St1 Deep Heat hydraulic stimulation in Espoo/Otaniemi, Finland. The README states that the CSV contains 1,977 earthquakes relocated with a double-difference technique, and local inspection confirmed fields for event ID, injection phase, UTC origin time, coordinates, altitude, local magnitude, moment magnitude, pick count and bootstrap uncertainty parameters. It is distinct from the broader Helsinki 2018 event-catalog row because it is a narrower derived product for source-parameter and relocation-uncertainty analysis.
openness: open GFZ Data Services record under CC BY-SA 4.0
source_type: data repository derived geothermal induced-seismicity catalog
evidence: GFZ landing page lists DOI 10.5880/GFZ.4.2.2019.001, CC BY-SA 4.0 and a direct datapub download directory. Local downloads confirmed 2019-001_Kwiatek-et-al_st1_parameters.csv (182,730 bytes) and README (2,079 bytes). CSV inspection counted 1,978 rows including header, i.e. 1,977 event rows, and README describes the same event count and column definitions.
scale: derived source-parameter catalog for an EGS stimulation sequence
feature_count: 1,977 relocated earthquakes confirmed by README and local CSV row count

name: Helsinki OTN-3 2018 fluid-induced seismicity waveform dataset
provider: GFZ Data Services / Holmgren, Kwiatek and Werner
theme_canonical: Enhanced geothermal induced seismicity waveform dataset
theme_raw: Seismic waveforms of fluid-induced seismicity from the 2018 hydraulic stimulation campaign at the OTN-3 well, Helsinki, Finland
coverage: Otaniemi, Espoo/Helsinki, southern Finland
formats: MiniSEED ZIP event waveforms, CSV event IDs, CSV station metadata, PDF data description
access: GFZ Data Services DOI landing page and direct datapub directory containing per-event MiniSEED ZIP files and CSV metadata
doi_or_citation: Holmgren, Kwiatek and Werner (2022), https://doi.org/10.5880/fidgeo.2022.012; related JGR Solid Earth article https://doi.org/10.1029/2022JB025226
time_span: June-July 2018 hydraulic stimulation campaign; inspected event IDs span July 2018 examples
notes: Distinct from both the Helsinki 2018 event catalog and the v022 source-parameter CSV. This record provides waveform packages for 507 selected fluid-induced earthquakes, plus event and station metadata, supporting empirical Green's function analysis of microseismic rupture behaviour.
desc_zh: 该 GFZ Data Services 数据集发布 Helsinki/Otaniemi OTN-3 井 2018 年水力刺激期间流体诱发地震的波形数据。GFZ 摘要说明数据包含 507 个地震事件，由周边 12 个三分量 4.5 Hz 地震台站记录，台站采样率为 500 Hz；本地抽查确认下载目录包含事件 ID CSV、台站 CSV 和每事件 MiniSEED ZIP 目录。它补充了已有事件目录，适合做波形、经验格林函数和微破裂行为分析。
desc_en: This GFZ Data Services dataset provides waveform data for fluid-induced earthquakes from the 2018 hydraulic stimulation campaign at the OTN-3 well in Helsinki/Otaniemi, Finland. The GFZ abstract states that it contains waveforms for 507 earthquakes recorded by a surrounding network of 12 three-component 4.5 Hz geophone stations sampled at 500 Hz; local inspection confirmed event-ID CSV, station CSV and a per-event MiniSEED ZIP directory. It complements the existing event catalogs by providing waveform material for empirical Green's function and microseismic rupture analyses.
openness: open GFZ Data Services record under CC BY 4.0
evidence: GFZ landing page lists DOI 10.5880/fidgeo.2022.012, CC BY 4.0 and direct datapub access. Local downloads confirmed Event_ids.csv (52,517 bytes, 508 rows including header, 507 event rows) and stations.csv (436 bytes, 13 rows including header, 12 stations). The MiniSEED directory index exposed per-event ZIP files matching the 507-event record.
scale: selected waveform dataset for an EGS stimulation sequence
feature_count: 507 earthquakes and 12 stations confirmed by local metadata inspection
file_size: Event_ids.csv 52,517 bytes; stations.csv 436 bytes; per-event MiniSEED ZIP files in datapub directory

name: Äspö Hard Rock Laboratory 2015 hydraulic stimulation seismicity and waveform dataset
provider: GFZ Data Services / GFZ German Research Centre for Geosciences / University of Potsdam / GmuG / SolExperts
theme_raw: Comprehensive data set of in-situ hydraulic stimulation experiments for geothermal purposes at the Äspö Hard Rock Laboratory
coverage: Äspö Hard Rock Laboratory, near Oskarshamn/Figeholm, Sweden
formats: CSV seismicity catalogs, waveform archives, TAR.GZ hydraulic/geological/metadata packages, PDF data description
access: GFZ Data Services DOI landing page and direct datapub directory with seismic_data, hydraulic_data, metadata and reports
doi_or_citation: Zang et al. (2023), https://doi.org/10.5880/GFZ.2.6.2023.004; related ESSD discussion https://doi.org/10.5194/essd-2023-170
time_span: May-June 2015 hydraulic fracture experiment; local protocol and catalogs inspected for 2015-06 event times
notes: Accepted as a mine-scale underground laboratory hydraulic-stimulation dataset rather than a field-scale EGS sequence. The GFZ abstract states that six tests with three injection schemes were performed and that both waveform data and seismicity catalogs are provided. Local inspection confirmed continuous acoustic-emission catalog and triggered injection-related event catalog files, plus metadata for AE sensors, accelerometers and broadband seismometers.
desc_zh: 该 GFZ Data Services 数据集发布瑞典 Äspö Hard Rock Laboratory 2015 年地下水力压裂/刺激实验的数据，目标是研究不同注入方案下的岩石破裂并控制伴随地震性。GFZ 页面说明实验包含 6 个测试和 3 种注入方案，水流、压力、渗透率、波形和地震目录均有数据；本地抽查确认 continuous AE 目录含 19,756 条事件，triggered/matched 目录含 196 条注入相关事件。该条目属于地下实验室/矿山尺度诱发声发射与微震数据，与场地级 EGS 目录尺度不同，但仍是明确的人为水力刺激诱发地震性开放数据。
desc_en: This GFZ Data Services dataset releases data from underground hydraulic fracturing/stimulation experiments at the Äspö Hard Rock Laboratory in Sweden in 2015, designed to test injection schemes that fracture rock while reducing or mitigating seismicity. The GFZ page states that six tests with three injection schemes were performed and that injection, pressure, permeability, waveform and seismicity catalog data are provided; local inspection confirmed a continuous AE catalog with 19,756 event rows and a triggered/matched catalog with 196 injection-related events. The row is classified as a mine-scale underground laboratory induced acoustic-emission/microseismic dataset rather than a field-scale EGS sequence.
openness: open GFZ Data Services record under CC BY 4.0
source_type: data repository in-situ hydraulic stimulation seismicity and waveform dataset
evidence: GFZ landing page lists DOI 10.5880/GFZ.2.6.2023.004, CC BY 4.0 and direct datapub access. The abstract states that seismicity was monitored in triggered and continuous mode by acoustic emission sensors, accelerometers and broadband seismometers and that waveform data and seismicity catalogs are provided. Local inspection found AE_triggered/CATALOG/aspo_catalog_kwiatek2018niemz2020_match.csv (15,275 bytes; 197 rows including header; 196 triggered injection-related events), AE_continuous/CATALOG/aspo_catalog_niemz2020.csv (1,521,243 bytes; 19,757 rows including header; 19,756 event rows), trigger.readme and continous.readme, and metadata for AE, accelerometer and seismometer sensors.
scale: mine-scale underground laboratory hydraulic stimulation experiment
feature_count: 19,756 continuous AE event rows and 196 triggered injection-related event rows confirmed by local CSV inspection
file_size: continuous catalog CSV 1,521,243 bytes; triggered catalog CSV 15,275 bytes; meta_data.tar.gz 7,619 bytes; hydraulic_data.tar.gz 13,769,461 bytes; data-description PDF 230,081 bytes

name: EGS Collab Experiment 2 microseismic monitoring waveform dataset
provider: Geothermal Data Repository / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory / DOE EGS Collab
theme_canonical: Enhanced geothermal induced microseismic waveform dataset
theme_raw: Continuous passive microseismic monitoring data from EGS Collab Experiment 2 stimulation and thermal circulation tests
coverage: Sanford Underground Research Facility, Lead, South Dakota, United States
formats: GDR Data Lake S3 binary waveform files, PDF monitoring report, S3/OEDI browser access
access: GDR landing page, OEDI S3 viewer, no-sign-request AWS S3 prefix and monitoring report download
doi_or_citation: Hopp, C. (2024). EGS Collab Experiment 2: Microseismic Monitoring. Geothermal Data Repository, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. https://doi.org/10.15121/2516752
time_span: February-September 2022 stimulation and thermal circulation tests; GDR record published 2024-05-28
notes: Distinct from active-source EGS Collab Experiment 2 CASSM data. This row captures passive microseismic monitoring during stimulation and thermal circulation tests. GDR describes 16 three-component accelerometers and a 24-channel hydrophone array installed in boreholes around the test area, with raw binary continuous waveform files named by timestamp. S3 listing confirmed separate stimulation and thermal_circulation prefixes under egs_collab/experiment_2/passive_seismic/.
desc_zh: 该 Geothermal Data Repository 数据集发布 EGS Collab Experiment 2 在 Sanford Underground Research Facility 的被动微震连续波形监测数据。GDR 页面说明实验在 2022 年 2-9 月的刺激和热循环测试期间采集数据，通过向 1200-1500 m 深度岩体注入高压流体来研究 EGS 过程并诱发微震；监测系统包括 16 个三分量加速度计和 24 通道水听器阵列。OEDI/GDR Data Lake 公开 S3 前缀提供 stimulation 和 thermal_circulation 两类原始二进制波形文件，并配有详细监测报告。
desc_en: This Geothermal Data Repository dataset releases passive continuous microseismic waveform monitoring data from EGS Collab Experiment 2 at the Sanford Underground Research Facility. The GDR page states that data were recorded during stimulation and thermal circulation tests from February to September 2022, as high-pressure fluid injection at 1200-1500 m depth was used to study EGS processes and induce microseismic events; the monitoring system included 16 three-component accelerometers and a 24-channel hydrophone array. The OEDI/GDR Data Lake exposes public S3 prefixes for stimulation and thermal_circulation raw binary waveform files, with an accompanying monitoring report.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: DOE geothermal data repository passive microseismic waveform dataset
evidence: GDR submission 1607 lists DOI 10.15121/2516752, public access, CC BY 4.0, continuous seismic waveform data, 16 three-component accelerometers, a 24-channel hydrophone array, and raw binary files named by timestamps. The GDR page links to an OEDI S3 viewer and reports AWS CLI access for s3://gdr-data-lake/egs_collab/experiment_2/passive_seismic/. S3 ListObjectsV2 confirmed stimulation/ and thermal_circulation/ prefixes. A sample stimulation file vbox_202203221750087104.dat is 819,200,150 bytes, and the monitoring report PDF is 15,908,887 bytes.
scale: underground EGS experiment passive waveform archive
feature_count: 16 three-component accelerometers and 24-channel hydrophone array; S3 prefixes for stimulation and thermal_circulation continuous waveform files
file_size: sample stimulation .dat file 819,200,150 bytes; monitoring report PDF 15,908,887 bytes; full S3 archive contains many timestamped binary waveform files

name: EGS Collab Experiment 1 microseismic monitoring waveform and manual catalog dataset
provider: Geothermal Data Repository / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory / DOE EGS Collab
theme_canonical: Enhanced geothermal induced microseismic waveform and event catalog dataset
theme_raw: Microseismic monitoring data, raw event waveform packages, station coordinates and manual microseismic catalogs from EGS Collab Experiment 1
coverage: Sanford Underground Research Facility 4850 Level, Lead, South Dakota, United States
formats: CSV manual event catalogs, TXT station coordinates, raw event waveform TAR/ZIP packages, Python helper script, PDF report, GDR/OEDI S3 browser
access: GDR landing page with direct file downloads and OEDI/GDR Data Lake S3 browser for experiment_1/microseismic_monitoring
doi_or_citation: Hopp, C. (2019). EGS Collab Experiment 1: Microseismic Monitoring. Geothermal Data Repository, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. https://doi.org/10.15121/1557417; related paper Schoenball et al. (2019), https://doi.org/10.1029/2019GL084982
time_span: May-June 2018 stimulation monitoring; GDR record published 2019-06-17 and updated 2020-07-13
notes: Distinct from EGS Collab active-source CASSM data and from later derived tomography products. This row captures the base passive microseismic monitoring archive for Experiment 1, including raw event waveform packages, station coordinates and manually picked event catalogs. Local inspection confirmed the latest manual catalog catalog_manual_all_v200629.csv has 1,932 event rows plus header and adds relative magnitude; the earlier v190312 catalog has 1,933 event rows plus header; station_coordinates.txt lists 36 station rows plus header.
desc_zh: 该 Geothermal Data Repository 数据集发布 DOE EGS Collab Experiment 1 在 Sanford Underground Research Facility 4850 Level 的被动微震监测数据。GDR 记录提供原始事件波形包、台站坐标、人工微震事件目录、Python 辅助脚本和论文/报告材料；本地检查确认新版人工目录含 1,932 条事件记录，字段包括事件时间、三维位置、定位误差、RMS 和相对震级。它是 EGS Collab Experiment 1 派生定位、速度模型和诱发微震研究的基础开放数据源。
desc_en: This Geothermal Data Repository dataset releases passive microseismic monitoring data from DOE EGS Collab Experiment 1 at the 4850 Level of the Sanford Underground Research Facility. The GDR record provides raw event waveform packages, station coordinates, manual microseismic event catalogs, a Python helper script and report/paper material; local inspection confirmed that the latest manual catalog contains 1,932 event rows with event time, 3D location, location errors, RMS and relative magnitude fields. It is the base open data source for EGS Collab Experiment 1 derived locations, velocity models and induced microseismicity studies.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: DOE geothermal data repository passive microseismic waveform and event catalog dataset
evidence: GDR submission 1166 lists public access, DOI 10.15121/1557417, CC BY 4.0, raw event waveform archives, manual catalog CSVs, station coordinates and OEDI S3 browser access. Local checks confirmed catalog_manual_all_v200629.csv is 258,075 bytes with 1,933 lines including header, catalog_manual_all_v190312.csv is 295,206 bytes with 1,934 lines including header, station_coordinates.txt is 1,539 bytes with 37 lines including header, eventfiles_raw.tar is 685,247,726 bytes and eventfiles_raw_long.zip is 3,121,910,744 bytes by HEAD.
scale: underground EGS experiment passive waveform and event catalog archive
feature_count: 1,932 events in latest manual catalog; 36 station rows in station coordinate file; raw short and long event waveform packages available
file_size: catalog_manual_all_v200629.csv 258,075 bytes; catalog_manual_all_v190312.csv 295,206 bytes; station_coordinates.txt 1,539 bytes; eventfiles_raw.tar 685,247,726 bytes; eventfiles_raw_long.zip 3,121,910,744 bytes

name: EGS Collab Experiment 1 transfer-learning double-difference microseismic catalog and velocity model
provider: Geothermal Data Repository / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory / DOE EGS Collab
theme_canonical: Enhanced geothermal induced microseismic relocated catalog and velocity model
theme_raw: 3D seismic velocity model and updated MEQ catalog using transfer-learning aided double-difference tomography for EGS Collab Experiment 1
coverage: Sanford Underground Research Facility 4850 Level, Lead, South Dakota, United States
formats: CSV updated MEQ catalog, CSV 3D Vp/Vs velocity model, MP4 velocity-model animations, interactive HTML location viewer
access: GDR landing page with direct CSV, HTML and MP4 downloads
doi_or_citation: Feng, Q. (2020). EGS Collab Experiment 1: 3D Seismic Velocity Model and Updated Microseismic Catalog Using Transfer-Learning Aided Double-Difference Tomography. Geothermal Data Repository, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. https://doi.org/10.15121/1632061; related article https://doi.org/10.1029/2020GL088651
time_span: Derived from 2018 EGS Collab Experiment 1 microseismic monitoring; GDR record published 2020-02-18
notes: Accepted as a derived product rather than a duplicate of the base monitoring archive because it provides an updated MEQ catalog and 3D velocity model generated with transfer-learning aided double-difference tomography. It is also distinct from GDR submission 1228: local inspection found a different DOI, different catalog row count and different relocated coordinates.
desc_zh: 该 GDR 数据集发布 EGS Collab Experiment 1 的迁移学习辅助双差层析成像成果，包括更新微震事件目录和三维 P/S 波速度模型。直接下载的 Updated_MEQ_catalog.csv 含 1,743 条事件记录，字段包括事件时间、三维坐标、x/y/z 误差和 RMS；3D_seismic_velocity_model.csv 提供规则网格的 Vp、Vs 及质量标记。它适合用于比较人工诱发微震重定位结果、速度结构和流体刺激过程。
desc_en: This GDR dataset releases transfer-learning aided double-difference tomography products for EGS Collab Experiment 1, including an updated microseismic earthquake catalog and a 3D P/S-wave velocity model. The directly downloadable Updated_MEQ_catalog.csv contains 1,743 event rows with event time, 3D coordinates, x/y/z errors and RMS fields, while 3D_seismic_velocity_model.csv provides gridded Vp, Vs and quality flags. It supports comparison of induced microseismic relocations, velocity structure and fluid-stimulation behavior.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: DOE geothermal data repository derived induced-seismicity catalog and velocity model
evidence: GDR submission 1214 lists public access, DOI 10.15121/1632061, CC BY 4.0, updated MEQ catalog, 3D seismic velocity model, animations and an interactive location viewer. Local checks confirmed Updated_MEQ_catalog.csv is 207,501 bytes with 1,744 lines including header, 3D_seismic_velocity_model.csv is 3,954,036 bytes by HEAD and begins with x, y, z, Vp, Vp Quality, Vs and Vs Quality fields, and Interactive_MEQ_locations.html is 333,281 bytes.
scale: underground EGS experiment derived microseismic catalog and velocity-model product
feature_count: 1,743 relocated microseismic event rows; gridded Vp/Vs velocity model CSV available

name: EGS Collab Experiment 1 double-difference microseismic catalog and velocity model
provider: Geothermal Data Repository / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory / DOE EGS Collab
theme_canonical: Enhanced geothermal induced microseismic relocated catalog and velocity model
theme_raw: 3D seismic velocity model and updated microseismic catalog from double-difference seismic tomography for EGS Collab Experiment 1
coverage: Sanford Underground Research Facility 4850 Level, Lead, South Dakota, United States
formats: CSV updated MEQ catalog, CSV 3D Vp/Vs velocity model, MP4 velocity-model animations, interactive HTML comparison viewer
access: GDR landing page with direct CSV, HTML and MP4 downloads
doi_or_citation: Feng, Q. (2020). EGS Collab Experiment 1: 3D Seismic Velocity Model and Updated Microseismic Catalog from Double-Difference Seismic Tomography. Geothermal Data Repository, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. https://doi.org/10.15121/1642468
time_span: Derived from 2018 EGS Collab Experiment 1 microseismic monitoring; GDR record published 2020-04-13
notes: Accepted as a second derived Experiment 1 product because it has its own GDR DOI and an updated MEQ catalog with a different event count and locations from the transfer-learning aided product in submission 1214. The record also provides original and updated velocity-model files and an interactive comparison viewer.
desc_zh: 该 GDR 数据集发布 EGS Collab Experiment 1 的双差地震层析成像更新目录和三维速度模型。直接检查确认 updated_MEQ_catalog.csv 含 1,859 条事件记录，字段包括事件时间、三维坐标、坐标误差和 RMS；页面还提供更新后的三维 Vp/Vs 模型、原始模型副本、动画和交互式位置比较 HTML。它与 1214 的迁移学习辅助版本是不同 DOI 和不同重定位结果的相邻派生产品。
desc_en: This GDR dataset releases double-difference seismic tomography products for EGS Collab Experiment 1, including an updated microseismic catalog and 3D velocity model. Direct inspection confirmed that updated_MEQ_catalog.csv contains 1,859 event rows with event time, 3D coordinates, coordinate errors and RMS fields; the record also provides updated and original 3D Vp/Vs model files, animations and an interactive location-comparison HTML file. It is a neighboring but distinct derived product from the transfer-learning aided submission 1214, with a different DOI and relocation result.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: DOE geothermal data repository derived induced-seismicity catalog and velocity model
evidence: GDR submission 1228 lists public access, DOI 10.15121/1642468, CC BY 4.0, updated MEQ catalog, updated and original 3D seismic velocity model files, animations and an interactive comparison viewer. Local checks confirmed updated_MEQ_catalog.csv is 221,305 bytes with 1,860 lines including header, 3D_seismic_velocity_model_updated.csv is 3,954,036 bytes by HEAD and begins with x, y, z, Vp, Vp Quality, Vs and Vs Quality fields, and Interactive_MEQ_location_comparison.html is 657,044 bytes.
scale: underground EGS experiment derived microseismic catalog and velocity-model product
feature_count: 1,859 relocated microseismic event rows; gridded updated Vp/Vs velocity model CSV available

name: Northern Montney Play 2014-2021 hydraulic-fracturing induced earthquake catalog
provider: Zenodo / Wang, Kao, Dokht, Visser and Yu
theme_raw: Earthquake catalog for northern Montney Play from 2014-2021 associated with controlling-factor analysis of hydraulic-fracturing induced seismicity
coverage: Northern Montney Play, northeastern British Columbia, Canada
formats: Whitespace/tab-delimited text catalog via Zenodo file download
access: Zenodo landing page and direct file download through Zenodo record API
doi_or_citation: Wang, B., Kao, H., Dokht, R. M. H., Visser, R. and Yu, H. (2022). Delineating the Controlling Factors of Hydraulic Fracturing-Induced Seismicity in the Northern Montney Play, Northeastern British Columbia, Canada, with Machine Learning. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6378188; related SRL article https://doi.org/10.1785/0220220075
notes: Accepted as a distinct broader northern Montney catalog rather than a duplicate of the Kiskatinaw catalog already in v026. The existing Kiskatinaw row represents a 2017-2020 high-resolution catalog for the Kiskatinaw/Dawson-Septimus area, whereas this Zenodo record describes an earthquake catalog for the northern Montney Play from 2014-2021 and has its own DOI, file, title and row count. The direct file has no extension and is whitespace/tab-delimited, with latitude, longitude, magnitude and date-time fields.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布加拿大不列颠哥伦比亚东北部 Northern Montney Play 2014-2021 年地震目录，用于 Wang 等关于水力压裂诱发地震控制因子的机器学习研究。记录页说明数据为 earthquake catalog，开放许可证为 CC BY 4.0；本地检查确认可下载文件 `finalcatalog` 含 3,169 条事件行，字段包括纬度、经度、震级和时间。它与已有 Kiskatinaw 2017-2020 高分辨率诱发地震目录不同，覆盖区域/时间窗更宽但字段更简洁。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases an earthquake catalog for the Northern Montney Play in northeastern British Columbia, Canada, from 2014 to 2021, supporting Wang et al.'s machine-learning study of controlling factors for hydraulic-fracturing induced seismicity. The record describes the file as an earthquake catalog and uses a CC BY 4.0 license; local inspection confirmed that the downloadable `finalcatalog` file contains 3,169 event rows with latitude, longitude, magnitude and date-time fields. It is distinct from the existing Kiskatinaw 2017-2020 high-resolution induced-earthquake catalog, with a broader area/time window but simpler fields.
openness: open Zenodo dataset under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: repository earthquake catalog for hydraulic-fracturing induced seismicity study
evidence: Zenodo API record 6378188 lists DOI 10.5281/zenodo.6378188, resource type Dataset, license cc-by-4.0, description 'Earthquake catalog for northern Montney Play from 2014-2021', and file finalcatalog. Local download confirmed finalcatalog is 139,093 bytes with 3,170 lines including header and 3,169 event rows; first fields are Latitude, Longitude, Mag. and Date.
scale: regional northern Montney Play hydraulic-fracturing induced seismicity catalog
feature_count: 3,169 event rows confirmed by local file inspection

name: Lake Pertusillo 2005-2006 reservoir-induced seismicity catalog
provider: Zenodo / Napolitano, Improta, Passarelli and Valoroso
theme_raw: High-precision double-difference catalog of small-magnitude reservoir-induced seismicity associated with the Pertusillo water reservoir
coverage: Lake Pertusillo / High Agri Valley, Southern Italy
formats: Plain text space/tab-delimited earthquake catalog
access: Zenodo landing page, Zenodo API metadata and direct text-file download
doi_or_citation: Napolitano, F., Improta, L., Passarelli, L. and Valoroso, L. (2023). Lake Pertusillo reservoir induced seismicity catalog (Southern Italy). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7951142
time_span: 2005-05-11 to 2006-06-12 passive survey period; Zenodo record published 2023-05-19
notes: Accepted as a reservoir-induced seismicity event catalog distinct from the existing Val d'Agri/INSIEME network and preliminary catalog rows in v027. The Zenodo description states that template matching produced a high-precision double-difference catalog of 5,068 earthquakes with ML -0.7 to 2.6 and MC 0.2, based on a 13-month passive survey with 46 stations and 408 hand-picked template earthquakes. Local file inspection counted 5,070 event rows plus a header in the downloadable text file, so the row records the inspected file count while noting the provider description.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布意大利南部 High Agri Valley / Pertusillo 水库附近 2005-2006 年水库诱发小震目录。记录页说明研究基于 13 个月密集被动观测和 46 个台站，使用 408 个手工挑选模板进行模板匹配，得到高精度双差定位目录；本地检查确认下载文件含 5,070 条事件行，字段包括 ID、纬度、经度、深度、发震时刻和 ML。它补充了已有 Val d'Agri 台网/初步目录条目，是明确的水库诱发地震目录。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases a reservoir-induced small-earthquake catalog for the Lake Pertusillo / High Agri Valley area of southern Italy during 2005-2006. The record states that a 13-month dense passive survey with 46 stations and 408 hand-picked template events was processed with template matching to produce a high-precision double-difference catalog; local inspection confirmed that the downloadable text file contains 5,070 event rows with ID, latitude, longitude, depth, origin time and ML fields. It complements the existing Val d'Agri network/preliminary-catalog rows as a specific reservoir-induced seismicity catalog.
openness: open Zenodo dataset under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
evidence: Zenodo API record 7951142 lists DOI 10.5281/zenodo.7951142, resource type Dataset, license cc-by-4.0, and direct file Pertusillo_Reservoir_Catalog.txt. The description states that template matching produced a final high-precision double-difference catalog of 5,068 earthquakes from a 13-month passive survey with 46 stations and 408 hand-picked templates. Local download confirmed Pertusillo_Reservoir_Catalog.txt is 305,228 bytes with 5,071 lines including header and 5,070 event rows, with fields ID, LAT, LONG, DEPTH, OTIME and ML.
scale: local reservoir-induced seismicity sequence catalog from dense passive survey
feature_count: 5,070 event rows confirmed by local file inspection; provider description states 5,068 earthquakes and 408 templates

name: The Geysers Prati-9 and Prati-29 multiple-event relocated locations dataset
provider: IG PAS Data Portal / Institute of Geophysics, Polish Academy of Sciences / Staszek, Rudziński and Kwiatek
theme_canonical: Geothermal induced seismicity relocated event locations
theme_raw: Locations of multiple events from Prati-9 and Prati-29 seismicity cluster at The Geysers geothermal field, California
coverage: The Geysers geothermal field, Prati-9 and Prati-29 clusters, California, United States
formats: CSV event locations, TXT data description, IG PAS Data Portal metadata, geodata directory
access: IG PAS Data Portal landing page and direct geodata.igf.edu.pl CSV/TXT downloads
doi_or_citation: IG PAS Data Portal DOI https://doi.org/10.25171/InstGeoph_PAS_IGData_seismology-data-2026-006; Staszek, Rudziński and Kwiatek (2021), https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-021-03267-y; later used by Staszek, Rudziński and Leptokaropoulos (2025), https://doi.org/10.1785/0220250001
time_span: derived from The Geysers Prati-9 and Prati-29 geothermal-field seismicity clusters; IG PAS files dated 2026-03
notes: Accepted as a derived open geothermal-induced seismicity location product, distinct from the v028 NCEDC Geysers CEC network/archive row. The dataset provides double-difference relocated multiple-event locations with cross-correlation and structure assignment for Prati-9 and Prati-29 clusters. The description notes NCEDC Enhanced Geothermal Systems event IDs, allowing linkage back to the source catalog.
desc_zh: 该 IG PAS Data Portal 数据集发布美国加州 The Geysers 地热田 Prati-9 和 Prati-29 地震簇的多重事件重定位位置。数据由 Staszek 等基于交叉相关双差重定位得到，并给出事件对应结构 A-D 的编号；本地检查确认 TG_ME.csv 为可直接下载的 CSV，含 202 条事件记录，字段包括 NCEDC 事件 ID、纬度、经度、深度和结构编号。它补充已有 Geysers 台网/波形归档，是面向流体迁移路径识别的开放派生目录。
desc_en: This IG PAS Data Portal dataset releases relocated multiple-event locations for the Prati-9 and Prati-29 seismicity clusters at The Geysers geothermal field in California. The locations were obtained by Staszek et al. using cross-correlation double-difference relocation and include structure assignments A-D; local inspection confirmed that TG_ME.csv is directly downloadable and contains 202 event records with NCEDC event ID, latitude, longitude, depth and structure index fields. It complements the existing Geysers network/waveform archive as an open derived catalog for fluid-migration-path analysis.
openness: open IG PAS Data Portal record under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: data portal derived geothermal induced-seismicity event-location dataset
evidence: IG PAS Data Portal page lists Dataset DOI 10.25171/InstGeoph_PAS_IGData_seismology-data-2026-006, CC BY 4.0 license and Access constraint Open. Local geodata directory inspection found TG_ME.csv and TG_ME_description.txt. TG_ME.csv is 7,699 bytes with 203 lines; after one dataset title line and one CSV header line it contains 202 event records. TG_ME_description.txt is 1,294 bytes and explains Event_id, Lat, Lon, Depth and Structure_idx fields.
scale: derived event-location dataset for two geothermal-field seismicity clusters
feature_count: 202 relocated event records confirmed by local CSV inspection

name: The Geysers Prati-9 and Prati-29 stress-drop probability-density dataset
provider: IG PAS Data Portal / Institute of Geophysics, Polish Academy of Sciences / Staszek, Orlecka-Sikora, Leptokaropoulos, Kwiatek and Martínez-Garzón
theme_raw: Stress drops and probability density functions of events from Prati-9 and Prati-29 seismicity cluster at The Geysers geothermal field, California
coverage: The Geysers geothermal field, Prati-9 and Prati-29 clusters, California, United States
formats: MAT MATLAB source-parameter table, TXT data description, IG PAS Data Portal metadata, geodata directory
access: IG PAS Data Portal landing page and direct geodata.igf.edu.pl MAT/TXT downloads
doi_or_citation: IG PAS Data Portal DOI https://doi.org/10.25171/InstGeoph_PAS_IGData_seismology-data-2026-007; Staszek et al. (2017), https://doi.org/10.1002/2017GL073929; Staszek, Rudziński and Kwiatek (2021), https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-021-03267-y; Staszek, Rudziński and Leptokaropoulos (2025), https://doi.org/10.1785/0220250001
time_span: derived from The Geysers Prati-9 and Prati-29 geothermal-field seismicity clusters; IG PAS files dated 2026-03
notes: Accepted as a separate source-parameter product from the Prati-9/Prati-29 location dataset because it has a distinct DOI and provides stress drops plus probability-density functions for 328 events. It is directly related to injection/geothermal induced seismicity at The Geysers and complements both the NCEDC source catalog and the v029 relocated-location row.
desc_zh: 该 IG PAS Data Portal 数据集发布 The Geysers 地热田 Prati-9 和 Prati-29 地震簇 328 个事件的静态应力降及其概率密度函数。说明文件指出这些参数由 mesh spectral ratio 技术计算，MAT 文件中的 TG_SD 表包含事件 ID、log10 应力降均值、概率密度函数横轴和概率密度值。本地检查确认数据目录开放，TG_SD.mat 可 HEAD 访问且大小为 503,782 bytes，说明文件为 1,730 bytes。
desc_en: This IG PAS Data Portal dataset releases static stress drops and probability-density functions for 328 events from the Prati-9 and Prati-29 seismicity clusters at The Geysers geothermal field. The description states that the parameters were calculated with the mesh spectral ratio technique, and that the TG_SD table in the MAT file contains event ID, mean log10 static stress drop, PDF x values and probability density values. Local inspection confirmed an open data directory, a reachable TG_SD.mat file of 503,782 bytes, and a 1,730-byte description file.
openness: open IG PAS Data Portal record under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: data portal derived geothermal induced-seismicity source-parameter dataset
evidence: IG PAS Data Portal page lists Dataset DOI 10.25171/InstGeoph_PAS_IGData_seismology-data-2026-007, CC BY 4.0 license and Access constraint Open. Local geodata directory inspection found TG_SD.mat and TG_SD_description.txt. HEAD confirmed TG_SD.mat is 503,782 bytes. TG_SD_description.txt is 1,730 bytes and states that the dataset contains stress drops and probability density functions for 328 events from Prati-9 and Prati-29, with fields ID, log10_SD, x_pdf and y_pdf in the TG_SD table.
scale: derived source-parameter dataset for two geothermal-field seismicity clusters
feature_count: 328 events stated by the provider description

name: UCI Seismic Bumps coal-mine seismic hazard dataset
provider: UCI Machine Learning Repository / Sikora and Wrobel / datasets GitHub mirror
theme_raw: Shift-level seismic and seismoacoustic monitoring summaries for forecasting high-energy seismic bumps in a Polish coal mine
coverage: Two longwalls in a Polish coal mine
license: CC BY 4.0 for UCI canonical dataset; GitHub mirror datapackage states ODC PDDL
formats: UCI ARFF zip, GitHub CSV mirror, Frictionless datapackage JSON
access: UCI Machine Learning Repository landing page and direct ARFF ZIP download; GitHub raw CSV/datapackage mirror
doi_or_citation: Sikora, M. and Wrobel, L. (2010). seismic-bumps [Dataset]. UCI Machine Learning Repository. https://doi.org/10.24432/C5W902
time_span: not specified by UCI; each row summarizes one 8-hour shift
notes: Accepted as a structured mining-induced seismic hazard dataset rather than an earthquake waveform catalog. UCI states that each row summarizes seismic activity in the rock mass during one 8-hour shift, and the target class indicates whether a high-energy seismic bump occurred in the next shift. The GitHub/datasets CSV mirror is convenient and tabular but has repeated rows removed, so it contains 2,578 data rows versus 2,584 in the UCI canonical ARFF file; this row treats UCI as canonical.
desc_zh: 该 UCI Machine Learning Repository 数据集记录波兰一座煤矿两条长壁工作面的矿山 seismic bump / rockburst 危险预测问题。每条记录汇总 8 小时 shift 内的地震和声发射/地震声学监测指标，包括 GMax geophone 能量、脉冲数、不同能量级 seismic bumps 数量、总能量和最大能量；目标类表示下一 shift 是否出现能量超过 10^4 J 的高能 seismic bump。本地检查确认 UCI 原始 ARFF 文件含 2,584 个实例、19 个属性，其中正类 170 个；它是人工采矿诱发震动/岩爆危险的经典开放结构化数据集。
desc_en: This UCI Machine Learning Repository dataset represents the seismic bump / rockburst hazard forecasting problem for two longwalls in a Polish coal mine. Each record summarizes seismic and seismoacoustic monitoring indicators over an 8-hour shift, including GMax geophone energy, pulse counts, counts of seismic bumps in different energy ranges, total energy and maximum energy; the target class indicates whether a high-energy seismic bump above 10^4 J occurred in the next shift. Local inspection confirmed that the canonical UCI ARFF file contains 2,584 instances and 19 attributes, with 170 positive hazardous-state examples, making it a classic open structured dataset for mining-induced seismic activity and hazard prediction.
openness: UCI public dataset under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0; GitHub mirror under ODC PDDL
source_type: machine-learning repository mining-induced seismic hazard dataset
evidence: UCI page lists DOI 10.24432/C5W902, CC BY 4.0 license, 2,584 instances, no missing values, and describes data from two longwalls in a Polish coal mine where each row summarizes seismic activity in an 8-hour shift. Local download of seismic+bumps.zip confirmed seismic-bumps.arff is 141,669 bytes, with 2,584 data rows after @data, 19 attributes and class counts 2,414 non-hazardous / 170 hazardous. The datasets GitHub mirror provides seismic-bumps.csv (127,523 bytes, 2,578 data rows after duplicate removal) and datapackage.json (7,024 bytes) with field descriptions and ODC PDDL license metadata.
scale: shift-level structured dataset for underground coal-mine seismic bump forecasting
feature_count: 2,584 canonical UCI instances; 19 attributes including target class; 170 positive hazardous-state examples
file_size: UCI seismic+bumps.zip 141,803 bytes; seismic-bumps.arff 141,669 bytes; GitHub CSV mirror 127,523 bytes; datapackage.json 7,024 bytes

name: Utah FORGE temperature-dependent fracture seismicity fluid-injection laboratory dataset
provider: Geothermal Data Repository / Pennsylvania State University / Wang, Elsworth and Eijsink
theme_canonical: Laboratory fluid-injection induced fracture seismicity dataset
theme_raw: Temperature-dependent fracture seismicity from fluid injection experiments on Utah FORGE granite samples
coverage: Pennsylvania State University laboratory experiments using Utah FORGE granite samples; Utah FORGE spatial metadata in GDR
formats: XLSX experimental data workbooks, PPTX experiment configuration and loading steps, GDR landing page
access: GDR landing page with direct downloadable XLSX and PPTX resource files
doi_or_citation: Wang, J., Elsworth, D. and Eijsink, A. (2024). Utah FORGE: Temperature-Dependent Fracture Seismicity from Fluid Injection Experiments. Geothermal Data Repository / Pennsylvania State University. https://doi.org/10.15121/2483881
time_span: laboratory experiments published 2024-02-29; three temperature settings 24 C, 78 C and 137 C
notes: Accepted as a laboratory-scale artificial seismicity / fracture seismicity dataset rather than a field earthquake catalog. GDR states that granite samples from Utah FORGE were prepared with a 30-degree inclined fracture and tested under controlled stress and temperature conditions while pore pressure was incrementally increased to simulate fluid injection. Local workbook inspection confirmed mechanical and pore-pressure time-series data; the 24 C workbook Readme states that seismic moment can be calculated every step based on the experimental data.
desc_zh: 该 Geothermal Data Repository 数据集发布使用 Utah FORGE 花岗岩样品开展的流体注入诱发断裂地震性实验数据。GDR 摘要说明样品带 30 度倾斜裂隙，在 24 C、78 C 和 137 C 三种温度、恒定围压与剪切应力条件下逐级升高孔隙压力以模拟流体注入；本地检查确认 3 个 XLSX 文件包含轴压、孔压、孔压流量、时间和剪切位移等时间序列。24 C 工作簿 Readme 明确说明这些实验数据可逐步计算 seismic moment，因此该条目补充了现场诱发地震目录之外的实验室尺度人工断裂地震性数据。
desc_en: This Geothermal Data Repository dataset releases fluid-injection induced fracture seismicity experiments conducted on Utah FORGE granite samples. The GDR abstract states that samples with a 30-degree inclined fracture were tested at 24 C, 78 C and 137 C under controlled confining pressure and shear stress while pore pressure was incrementally increased to simulate fluid injection; local inspection confirmed that the three XLSX files contain time series of axial pressure, pore pressure, pore-pressure flow rate, time and shearing displacement. The 24 C workbook Readme explicitly states that seismic moment can be calculated step by step from the experimental data, making this a laboratory-scale artificial fracture-seismicity dataset that complements field induced-earthquake catalogs.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: DOE geothermal data repository laboratory fluid-injection fracture seismicity dataset
evidence: GDR submission 1696 lists public access, DOI 10.15121/2483881, CC BY 4.0 and four direct resources. Local HEAD checks confirmed Experimental Data 137C XLSX 103,354 bytes, Experimental Data 24C XLSX 757,652 bytes, Experimental Data 78C XLSX 111,460 bytes and experiment-configuration PPTX 3,578,986 bytes. Local workbook inspection found 137C Sheet1 with 2,284 rows x 7 columns, 24C data sheet with 20,260 rows x 7 columns plus a Readme sheet, and 78C Sheet1 with 2,425 rows x 7 columns. Common fields include Axial pressure/kPa, Pore pressure/kPa, Flow rate for pore pressure/ml/min, Time/h, Time/m, Time/s and Shearing displacement/mm.
feature_count: three temperature-condition workbooks: 137C 2,284 rows; 24C 20,260 data rows plus Readme; 78C 2,425 rows
file_size: 137C XLSX 103,354 bytes; 24C XLSX 757,652 bytes; 78C XLSX 111,460 bytes; configuration PPTX 3,578,986 bytes

name: Utah FORGE fluid-injection-rate fault-reactivation acoustic-emission laboratory dataset
provider: Geothermal Data Repository / Pennsylvania State University / Elsworth, Eijsink, Marone, Shokouhi, Riviere, Wang and Yu
theme_canonical: Laboratory fluid-injection induced fault reactivation acoustic-emission dataset
theme_raw: Fluid injection-rate controls on seismic moment from laboratory fault reactivation experiments
coverage: Pennsylvania State University laboratory shear-reactivation experiments using Utah FORGE granitoid cores; Utah FORGE spatial metadata in GDR
formats: MAT processed acoustic-emission catalogs, TXT raw mechanical time-series, MATLAB processing scripts, GDR landing page
access: GDR landing page with direct downloadable MAT, TXT and MATLAB resource files
doi_or_citation: Elsworth, D., Eijsink, A., Marone, C., Shokouhi, P., Riviere, J., Wang, J. and Yu, J. (2025). Utah FORGE: Fluid Injection-Rate Controls on Seismic Moment from Laboratory Fault Reactivation Experiments. Geothermal Data Repository / Pennsylvania State University. https://gdr.openei.org/submissions/1775
time_span: laboratory experiments published 2025-09-16; GDR record last updated 2025-10-09
notes: Accepted as an expanded laboratory-scale artificial seismicity dataset for fluid-injection induced fault reactivation. GDR submission 1775 contains raw mechanical data and acoustic-emission recordings from fifteen shear reactivation experiments on 2.5-3 inch Utah FORGE granitoid cores, spanning injection rates of 0.05, 0.25 and 0.75 mL/min, prestress levels of about 60%, 80% and 90%, and uniform/non-uniform pore-pressure distributions. GDR 1625 is recorded as an earlier DOI-backed subset with three experiments and six files; v032 keeps 1775 as the broader canonical row to avoid duplicate catalog inflation.
desc_zh: 该 Geothermal Data Repository 数据集发布 Utah FORGE 花岗岩岩心在实验室中进行流体注入诱发断层剪切再活化实验的机械与声发射数据。GDR 说明共有 15 个实验，样品为带单一倾斜粗糙裂隙的 2.5-3 英寸 granitoid cores，注入速率覆盖 0.05、0.25 和 0.75 mL/min，并包含均匀与非均匀孔压分布、不同预应力水平下的剪切滑移过程。资源层面包含 14 个处理后的声发射 catalog MAT 文件、15 个原始机械 TXT 时间序列文件和 2 个 MATLAB 脚本，可用于研究注入速率、孔压扩散、剪切滑移速度与 seismic moment / acoustic emissions 之间的关系，是现场诱发地震目录之外的重要实验室人工地震数据源。
desc_en: This Geothermal Data Repository dataset releases mechanical and acoustic-emission data from laboratory fluid-injection fault-reactivation experiments on Utah FORGE granitoid cores. The GDR record describes fifteen shear-reactivation experiments on 2.5-3 inch cores with a single inclined rough fracture, using injection rates of 0.05, 0.25 and 0.75 mL/min under uniform and non-uniform pore-pressure distributions and several prestress levels. The resources include 14 processed acoustic-emission catalog MAT files, 15 raw mechanical TXT time-series files and 2 MATLAB scripts, supporting analysis of injection rate, pore-pressure distribution, shear slip velocity, seismic moment and acoustic emissions as a laboratory-scale artificial seismicity dataset.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: DOE geothermal data repository laboratory fluid-injection fault-reactivation acoustic-emission dataset
evidence: GDR submission 1775 lists public access, CC BY 4.0 licensing, Pennsylvania State University as organization and 31 downloadable resources totaling 5.43 GiB on the page. Local link extraction and HEAD checks confirmed 31 direct resource URLs: 14 AEcatalog MAT files, 15 raw mechanical TXT files and 2 MATLAB scripts, with summed Content-Length 5,826,840,122 bytes. Sample TXT range checks found numeric 16-column raw mechanical time-series rows. Sample MATLAB script inspection confirmed fields such as pore/confining/axial pressures, pump volumes, flow rates, axial displacement, downstream pressure and acoustic-event loading/plotting routines. The GDR description states that acoustic emissions were recorded using P-wave transducers and compared against seismic moment and shear slip velocity.
scale: laboratory-scale fluid-injection induced fault reactivation and acoustic-emission experiment
feature_count: 15 laboratory shear reactivation experiments; 14 processed AE catalog MAT files, 15 raw mechanical TXT files and 2 MATLAB scripts confirmed by direct GDR link extraction
file_size: GDR page reports Download All Resources 5.43 GB / 31 files; local HEAD Content-Length sum 5,826,840,122 bytes across 31 direct resource URLs

name: Utah FORGE laboratory fault reactivation permeability and acoustic-emission dataset
provider: Geothermal Data Repository / Pennsylvania State University / Elsworth, Yu, Wang, Eijsink and Marone
theme_canonical: Laboratory fluid-injection induced fault reactivation acoustic-emission and permeability dataset
theme_raw: Laboratory fault reactivation and permeability evolution during fluid injection
coverage: Pennsylvania State University laboratory experiments on Westerly and Utah FORGE granitoid granite samples; Utah FORGE project context in GDR
formats: XLSX compiled mechanical measurements, ZIP raw acoustic-emission data packages, GDR landing page
access: GDR landing page with direct downloadable XLSX and ZIP resource files
doi_or_citation: Elsworth, D., Yu, P., Wang, J., Eijsink, A. and Marone, C. (2025). Utah FORGE: Laboratory Fault Reactivation and Permeability Evolution During Fluid Injection. Geothermal Data Repository / Pennsylvania State University. https://gdr.openei.org/submissions/1774
time_span: laboratory experiments published 2025-09-15; GDR record last updated 2025-10-09
notes: Accepted as a laboratory-scale artificial seismicity dataset because the GDR record explicitly documents fluid-injection reactivation of pre-loaded laboratory faults and includes mechanical measurements plus raw calibrated acoustic-emission data. It is distinct from v032 GDR 1775: v033 GDR 1774 emphasizes permeability evolution, stepwise upstream pore-pressure increments, Westerly/granitoid granite samples and raw AE waveform packages for PL02, PL03, PL14 and PL16 runs.
desc_zh: 该 Geothermal Data Repository 数据集发布流体注入诱发实验室断层再活化过程中渗透率演化、剪切位移和声发射响应的数据。GDR 说明实验使用带单一倾斜裂隙的 Westerly 与 Utah FORGE granitoid granite 圆柱样品，断层预加载至接近破裂状态后通过逐级升高上游孔隙压力再活化，并记录机械与地震学响应。本地检查确认其 XLSX 机械数据包含 5 个实验/运行工作表、合计 128,386 行，字段包括轴压、上游压力、泵体积、流量、时间和水平位移；另有 5 个大型 ZIP 原始 AE 数据包，适合研究流体注入、渗透率变化与诱发微震/声发射之间的联系。
desc_en: This Geothermal Data Repository dataset releases permeability, shear-displacement and acoustic-emission observations from laboratory fluid-injection fault-reactivation experiments. The GDR record states that cylindrical Westerly and Utah FORGE granitoid granite samples with a single inclined fracture were pre-loaded near failure and reactivated by stepwise upstream pore-pressure increases while mechanical and seismic responses were recorded. Local workbook inspection confirmed five experiment/run sheets with 128,386 rows of mechanical measurements, including axial pressure, upstream pressure, pump volumes, flow rates, time and horizontal displacement; five large ZIP packages provide raw AE data for PL02, PL03, PL14 and PL16 runs.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: DOE geothermal data repository laboratory fluid-injection fault-reactivation acoustic-emission dataset
evidence: GDR submission 1774 lists public access, CC BY 4.0 licensing, Pennsylvania State University as organization and six direct resources. Local HEAD checks confirmed the mechanical XLSX at 6,352,055 bytes and five AE ZIP packages: PL02 41,746,728,184 bytes, PL03 52,511,058,397 bytes, PL14 35,766,588,547 bytes, PL16 run 1 35,183,300,636 bytes and PL16 run 3 30,587,096,513 bytes; summed direct resource size is 195,801,124,332 bytes. Local XLSX inspection found sheets 02, 03, 14, 16_run1 and 16_run3 with 75,612, 37,017, 5,584, 6,078 and 4,095 rows respectively and 10 columns each.
scale: laboratory-scale fluid-injection induced fault reactivation, permeability evolution and acoustic-emission experiment
feature_count: five experiment/run sheets in the compiled mechanical workbook: 128,386 rows total; five raw AE ZIP packages for PL02, PL03, PL14, PL16 run 1 and PL16 run 3
file_size: six direct resources; local HEAD Content-Length sum 195,801,124,332 bytes (about 182.35 GiB)

name: Xinfengjiang Reservoir induced-earthquake catalog and waveform dataset
provider: Zenodo / Lipeng He
theme_canonical: Reservoir-induced seismicity catalog and waveform dataset
theme_raw: Long-time delays in induced earthquakes caused by low-permeability bodies: Xinfengjiang Reservoir, China
coverage: Xinfengjiang Reservoir, Guangdong, China
formats: RAR archives containing Xinfengjiang study data and catalog-earthquake waveforms, Zenodo landing page and API file records
access: Zenodo landing page with two direct downloadable RAR archives
doi_or_citation: He, L. (2025). Long-time Delays in Induced Earthquakes Caused by Low-Permeability Bodies: Insights from the Xinfengjiang Reservoir, China (v3). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.15494372; associated paper DOI: 10.1029/2024JB030579
time_span: dataset version accepted 2025-05-23; waveforms of catalog earthquakes from 2014 to 2018
notes: Accepted as a reservoir-induced seismicity dataset because the Zenodo record is an open dataset, explicitly names induced earthquakes at the Xinfengjiang Reservoir, and v3 adds waveforms of catalog earthquakes from 2014 to 2018. The landing page is sparse, so the row keeps the description tied to the verified metadata and file inventory rather than inferring event counts from the paper.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集对应新丰江水库诱发地震长期延迟机制研究，提供面向中国广东新丰江水库的开放数据包。Zenodo v3 页面明确说明新增了 2014-2018 年 catalog earthquakes 的波形，并提供 `Data_for_Xinfengjiang_R1.rar` 与 `Events_2014_2018.rar` 两个可下载压缩包，总大小约 11.31 GB。由于页面未给出事件数和详细字段，本目录仅将其标注为水库诱发地震目录/波形数据源，并把更细粒度信息留待后续解包审查。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset supports a study of long-time delays in induced earthquakes at the Xinfengjiang Reservoir in Guangdong, China. The v3 record explicitly states that it adds waveforms of catalog earthquakes from 2014 to 2018 and provides two downloadable archives, `Data_for_Xinfengjiang_R1.rar` and `Events_2014_2018.rar`, totaling about 11.31 GB. Because the landing page does not expose event counts or detailed column definitions, this row conservatively records it as an open reservoir-induced earthquake catalog/waveform source pending future archive-level inspection.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: Zenodo reservoir-induced seismicity catalog and waveform dataset
evidence: Zenodo record 15494372 is marked Dataset / Open, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.15494372, CC BY 4.0, published as v3 on 2025-05-23. The page states that this version adds waveforms of catalog earthquakes from 2014 to 2018. Zenodo API confirmed two files: Events_2014_2018.rar at 4,881,865,863 bytes and Data_for_Xinfengjiang_R1.rar at 6,430,951,979 bytes, with total direct file size 11,312,817,842 bytes.
scale: reservoir-induced seismicity catalog and waveform archive
feature_count: two RAR archives; one archive explicitly named Events_2014_2018 and one Xinfengjiang study-data archive
file_size: two Zenodo files totaling 11,312,817,842 bytes (about 10.54 GiB); Zenodo page reports 11.3 GB

name: Heterogeneous shear-vein reservoir-stimulation induced-seismicity numerical dataset
provider: Zenodo / University of Manchester / Ling, Kong, Yang and Shang
theme_raw: Reactivation of heterogeneous shear veins during reservoir stimulation: fracture interaction, stress perturbation and induced seismicity
coverage: Numerical DEM reservoir-stimulation model dataset; University of Manchester / Computers and Geotechnics study
formats: XLSX injection pressure, fault stress, pore pressure and source-mechanism tables; ZIP fracture, scalar-data and calibration-code packages; Zenodo landing page
access: Zenodo landing page with six direct downloadable XLSX/ZIP files
doi_or_citation: Ling, F., Kong, L., Yang, S. and Shang, J. (2025). Reactivation of Heterogeneous Shear Veins During Reservoir Stimulation: Fracture Interaction, Stress Perturbation, and Induced Seismicity. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.15069561; associated article DOI: 10.1016/j.compgeo.2025.107576
time_span: Zenodo v2 published 2025-03-22; associated article published 2025
notes: Accepted as a numerical/model artificial seismicity dataset, not as an observed earthquake catalog. The record is useful for the atlas because it exposes source-mechanism, pore-pressure, stress, injection-pressure, fracture and scalar data for a reservoir-stimulation induced-seismicity model. The row is marked with numerical/model scope to keep it separate from field catalogs and laboratory AE recordings.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布《Computers and Geotechnics》论文中关于储层刺激、异质剪切脉体再活化与诱发地震性的数值数据。Manchester 论文页说明研究在 DEM 框架中进行全矩张量反演与分解，并模拟流体注入含异质脉体的简化断层带，以分析水力裂缝与剪切脉体相互作用时的应力扰动和 source mechanism。数据文件包含注入压力、断层应力与孔压、source mechanism、fracture data、scalar data 和微参数校准代码；本地检查确认多个 XLSX 表含 19,801 行时间序列和 97 行 source-mechanism 数据。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases numerical data for a Computers and Geotechnics study of reservoir stimulation, heterogeneous shear-vein reactivation and induced seismicity. The University of Manchester publication page states that the work uses DEM-based full moment-tensor inversion/decomposition and injects fluids into a simplified fault-zone model containing heterogeneous veins to analyze stress perturbation and source mechanisms during hydraulic-fracture interaction. The downloadable files include injection pressure, fault stress and pore pressure, source mechanism, fracture data, scalar data and calibration code; local inspection confirmed XLSX time-series tables with 19,801 rows and a 97-row source-mechanism table.
openness: publicly accessible under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: Zenodo reservoir-stimulation induced-seismicity numerical model dataset
evidence: Zenodo record 15069561 is marked Dataset / Open, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.15069561 and CC BY 4.0. Zenodo API confirmed six files totaling 4,332,161 bytes: scalar data.zip, source mechnism.xlsx, Codes for micro parameterscalibration.zip, fracture data.zip, Injection pressure.xlsx and fault stress and pore pressure.xlsx. Local inspection found Injection pressure.xlsx with 19,801 rows x 3 columns, fault stress and pore pressure.xlsx with 19,801 rows x 9 columns, source mechnism.xlsx with 97 rows x 6 columns, fracture data.zip with six fracture-data workbooks and scalar data.zip with six scalar-data workbooks.
feature_count: six files; inspected tables include two 19,801-row time series, one 97-row source-mechanism table, six fracture-data workbooks and six scalar-data workbooks
file_size: six Zenodo files totaling 4,332,161 bytes; Zenodo page reports 4.3 MB

name: Rangely earthquake control experiment scanned develocorder recordings, 1972
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Earthquake Hazards Program / Rubinstein and Wang
theme_canonical: Fluid-injection earthquake-control experiment analog seismic recordings
theme_raw: Scanned recordings of the Rangely, Colorado earthquake control experiment, 1972
coverage: Rangely oil field, Colorado, USA
formats: Globus-hosted scanned develocorder image ZIP folders, station-location CSV, README DOCX, FGDC XML, USGS/ScienceBase landing pages
access: USGS data-release landing page, ScienceBase metadata/files, and Globus Access Portal for large scanned image files
doi_or_citation: Rubinstein, J.L., and Wang, K. (2025). Scanned Recordings of the Rangely, Colorado (USA) Earthquake Control Experiment (1972). U.S. Geological Survey data release. https://doi.org/10.5066/P9E1A90X
notes: Accepted as a field-scale artificial seismicity data source because the Rangely oil-field experiment deliberately varied fluid pressure to control induced earthquakes, and this USGS release preserves the analog seismic recordings from the experiment. The USGS page states that the large scanned data files are provided through a Globus Access Portal rather than standard download protocols.
