The ScienceBase API exposes station, README and FGDC metadata files; the README describes monthly seismogram folders, daily ZIP files and scanned images with WWVB time-code information.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布保存 Rangely 油田地震控制实验 1972 年的扫描 develocorder 胶片记录，是经典注水/回采调控孔压以诱发或抑制地震活动的现场实验数据。USGS 页面说明实验发生在 1969-1974 年 Colorado Rangely 周边，数据由 12 个台站、14 个通道记录并遥测至 Menlo Park；1972 年大体量扫描图像通过 Globus Access Portal 提供。README 本地检查确认数据按月文件夹组织，每日 zip 内包含连续胶片图像，并可利用 WWVB 时间码做精确定时；station CSV 给出站点经纬度、胶片通道和增益/特殊条件。
desc_en: This USGS data release preserves scanned develocorder film recordings from the 1972 portion of the Rangely oil-field earthquake-control experiment, a classic field experiment in which fluid pressure was varied to control induced seismicity. The USGS page states that the 1969-1974 Rangely experiment was recorded by 12 stations with 14 channels and telemetered to Menlo Park, and that the large 1972 scanned-image files are provided through a Globus Access Portal. Local README inspection confirmed monthly seismogram folders, daily ZIP files containing sequential scanned film images, WWVB time-code information for precise timing, and a station CSV with station coordinates, film channels and gain/special-condition notes.
openness: publicly accessible USGS data release marked CC0 1.0 / public-domain
source_type: USGS ScienceBase field-scale fluid-injection induced-seismicity analog recording data release
evidence: USGS data page and Science Data Catalog identify the record as a public data release with DOI 10.5066/P9E1A90X, induced-seismicity keyword, Rangely Colorado place keyword, 1971-12-31 to 1972-12-31 data dates, and CC0 rights. ScienceBase API exposed Rangely_stalocs.csv, 1972_Rangely_README.docx and 1972metadata.xml. Local README inspection found monthly folders, daily ZIP files, multiple sequential scanned images per day, 2000-pixel overlap between images, daily film-change gaps, and WWVB time-code recording. Local station CSV inspection found 14 station rows with coordinates and film-channel mappings.
scale: field-scale oil-field fluid-pressure earthquake-control experiment analog seismic recordings
feature_count: 12 stations / 14 channels according to USGS page and README; station CSV lists 14 station rows; large scanned data organized by month and daily ZIP files
file_size: large scanned data provided via Globus; ScienceBase metadata files inspected locally: station CSV 553 bytes, README DOCX 694,097 bytes, FGDC XML 12,515 bytes

name: Rangely earthquake control experiment scanned develocorder recordings, 1973
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Earthquake Hazards Program / Rubinstein and Wang
theme_canonical: Fluid-injection earthquake-control experiment analog seismic recordings
theme_raw: Scanned recordings of the Rangely earthquake control experiment, 1973
coverage: Rangely oil field, Colorado, USA
formats: Scanned develocorder image ZIP folders described by README, station-location CSV, README DOCX, FGDC XML, USGS/ScienceBase landing pages
access: USGS data-release landing page with ScienceBase metadata and downloadable station/README/XML files; scanned recording structure documented in README
doi_or_citation: Rubinstein, J.L., and Wang, K. (2024). Scanned Recordings of the Rangely Earthquake Control Experiment (1973). U.S. Geological Survey data release. https://doi.org/10.5066/P9NH4RM5
notes: Accepted as the 1973 companion release for the Rangely controlled fluid-pressure experiment. The USGS page and Science Data Catalog explicitly describe scanned recordings from the 1969-1974 Rangely earthquake control experiment and list public access, induced-seismicity keywording and CC0/public-domain rights. Unlike the 1972 page, the inspected ScienceBase JSON did not expose a Globus weblink, so the access notes distinguish verified metadata/README downloads from the scanned-image structure documented in the README.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布保存 Rangely 地震控制实验 1973 年的扫描 develocorder 胶片记录，延续 1972 年数据，记录注水/回采调控孔压期间的人工诱发地震观测。USGS 页面说明数据来自 12 个台站、14 个通道并遥测至 Menlo Park，Science Data Catalog 标注 induced seismicity、public access 和 CC0/public-domain 权利。README 本地检查确认 1973 年数据同样按月文件夹和每日 zip 组织，zip 内为连续扫描图像，并记录 WWVB 时间码；station CSV 还标明 1973 年 11 月 15 日前后部分台站停用和通道重排。
desc_en: This USGS data release preserves scanned develocorder film recordings from the 1973 portion of the Rangely earthquake-control experiment, continuing the observations of induced seismicity during controlled fluid-pressure changes. The USGS page states that the data were recorded by 12 stations with 14 channels and telemetered to Menlo Park, while the Science Data Catalog marks the release with induced-seismicity keywords, public access and CC0/public-domain rights. Local README inspection confirmed monthly folders, daily ZIP files of sequential scanned images and WWVB time-code information; the station CSV also documents station removals and channel reassignments around 1973-11-15.
openness: publicly accessible USGS data release marked CC0 1.0 / public-domain
source_type: USGS ScienceBase field-scale fluid-injection induced-seismicity analog recording data release
evidence: USGS data page and Science Data Catalog identify the record as a public data release with DOI 10.5066/P9NH4RM5, induced-seismicity keyword, Rangely Colorado place keyword, 1972-12-31 to 1973-12-31 data dates, and CC0/public-domain rights. ScienceBase API exposed Rangely_stalocs.csv, README.docx and 1973metadata.xml. Local README inspection found monthly folders, daily ZIP files, multiple sequential scanned images, 2000-pixel overlap, daily film-change gaps and WWVB time-code recording. Local station CSV inspection found film-channel mappings before and after 1973-11-15, with notes that COB, DHS and SWC were decommissioned in November 1973.
scale: field-scale oil-field fluid-pressure earthquake-control experiment analog seismic recordings
feature_count: 12 stations / 14 channels according to USGS page and README; station CSV lists station rows and pre/post 1973-11-15 channel mappings; scanned data organized by month and daily ZIP files according to README
file_size: ScienceBase metadata files inspected locally: station CSV 826 bytes, README DOCX 167,793 bytes, FGDC XML 12,088 bytes; scanned-image package size not enumerated in inspected ScienceBase API

name: Low-pressure long-term injection induced-seismicity sequence catalog compilation
provider: Springer Nature supplementary material / Watkins, Verdon and Rodríguez-Pradilla
theme_canonical: Multi-site low-pressure long-term fluid-injection induced-seismicity catalog compilation
theme_raw: The temporal evolution of induced seismicity sequences generated by low-pressure, long-term fluid injection
coverage: Multi-region compilation of wastewater disposal, natural-gas storage and carbon-capture/storage induced seismicity sequences, including North America, Europe, China and Colombia case studies
license: CC BY 4.0 article license; supplementary files distributed with open-access Springer Nature article
formats: ZIP supplementary data package with DAT event catalogs; PDF supplementary material; Springer article landing page
access: Open-access article page with direct downloadable supplementary ZIP and PDF files hosted by Springer static-content
doi_or_citation: Watkins, T.J.M., Verdon, J.P. and Rodríguez-Pradilla, G. (2023). The temporal evolution of induced seismicity sequences generated by low-pressure, long-term fluid injection. Journal of Seismology 27, 243-259. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10950-023-10141-z
time_span: case-specific event catalogs spanning historical wastewater-disposal, natural-gas-storage and CCS induced sequences; article published 2023-03-16
notes: Accepted as a cross-case digital catalog compilation rather than a single monitoring network. The article states that it compiles cases where seismicity was induced by wastewater disposal, natural-gas storage and carbon capture/storage, and that digital event catalogues are provided in the supplementary material. Local ZIP inspection confirmed 27 site-specific DAT catalogs with injection start/end date blocks and event rows. Because some source catalogs are already represented elsewhere in the atlas, this row is treated as a derived harmonized compilation useful for temporal-evolution and traffic-light-system analyses, not as a replacement for canonical provider catalogs.
desc_zh: 该开放补充数据包汇编了低压、长期、大体积流体注入诱发地震序列，用于分析 WWD、NGS 和 CCS 等场景下事件震级随注入过程的时间演化。Springer 论文页面说明研究筛选公开可用且事件数足够的地震目录，并在 supplementary material 中提供 digital event catalogues；本地检查确认 ZIP 中有 27 个 `*_Catalog.dat` 文件，每个文件含注入起止日期块和事件目录。目录覆盖 Cogdell、Paradox、Rongchang、Hutubi、Castor、Gaitan 等案例，本地按日期行计数约 30,553 个事件，是跨案例研究 magnitude jumps、trailing events 和诱发地震交通灯方案的重要汇编数据源。
desc_en: This open supplementary data package compiles low-pressure, long-term fluid-injection induced seismicity sequences for analyzing temporal magnitude evolution in wastewater-disposal, natural-gas-storage and CCS settings. The Springer article states that the study selected publicly available earthquake catalogs with enough events and provides digital event catalogues in the supplementary material; local ZIP inspection confirmed 27 `*_Catalog.dat` files, each with injection start/end date blocks and event-catalog rows. The compilation includes cases such as Cogdell, Paradox, Rongchang, Hutubi, Castor and Gaitan, with a local date-row count of about 30,553 events, making it a useful harmonized source for studying magnitude jumps, trailing events and induced-seismicity traffic-light assumptions across cases.
openness: open-access article and supplementary materials under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 article license
source_type: peer-reviewed supplementary multi-site injection-induced seismicity catalog compilation
evidence: Springer article page is open access, published 2023-03-16, and states that the study compiles WWD, NGS and CCS induced-seismicity cases and provides digital event catalogues in the Supplementary Material. Direct supplementary ZIP URL returned HTTP 200 with Content-Length 265,762 bytes and application/zip; direct supplementary PDF returned HTTP 200 with Content-Length 5,825,603 bytes. Local ZIP inspection found 57 members, including 27 real `Catalogues/*_Catalog.dat` files plus macOS metadata. Local parsing counted 30,553 event rows across the 27 DAT catalogs and confirmed fields such as Date, Time, Lat, Lon and Mag with injection start/end date blocks.
scale: multi-site wastewater-disposal, natural-gas-storage and CCS low-pressure long-term injection induced-seismicity compilation
feature_count: 27 site-specific DAT catalogs; local parsed event-row count 30,553
file_size: supplementary ZIP 265,762 bytes; supplementary PDF 5,825,603 bytes

name: Neuquén Basin / Vaca Muerta hydraulic-fracturing induced seismicity and stimulation dataset
provider: Zenodo / GitHub / Schultz and coauthors
theme_canonical: Hydraulic-fracturing induced seismicity catalog and stimulation-well dataset
theme_raw: Vaca Muerta induced earthquake and hydraulic-fracturing data package for VM-ID
coverage: Neuquén Basin / Vaca Muerta shale play, Argentina
license: CC BY 4.0 for Zenodo release; GitHub repository includes GPL-3.0 license for code
formats: CSV earthquake catalogs and hydraulic-fracturing well tables, MATLAB MAT processed data, MATLAB scripts, Zenodo archive, GitHub repository
access: GitHub repository with direct raw CSV/MAT/script downloads; Zenodo DOI archive listed in citation/evidence
doi_or_citation: RyanJamesSchultz/VM-ID: Seismica release. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14065345; associated open repository https://github.com/RyanJamesSchultz/VM-ID
time_span: earthquake catalog rows inspected from 2014 onward; repository release archived 2024-11-12
notes: Accepted because the Zenodo/GitHub package exposes real data files for Vaca Muerta induced-seismicity analysis, not only code. The Zenodo landing/archive was intermittently slow from the local network during v037 verification, so the stable catalog URL is the GitHub repository; the Zenodo DOI archive remains recorded in citation/evidence. GitHub raw URLs were locally inspected and lychee-verified. The row is scoped to the data package for induced-seismicity/hydraulic-fracturing analysis in the Neuquén Basin, with code and processed MAT files as supporting materials.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo/GitHub 数据包面向阿根廷 Neuquén Basin / Vaca Muerta 页岩区的水力压裂诱发地震识别与分析，包含地震目录、压裂井和非常规井数据。GitHub `data/` 目录本地检查确认 `20240314_VM_quakes_all_soruces.csv` 有 371 行地震记录，字段包括来源、本地日期/时间、纬度、经度、深度、ML、震中/深度误差和烈度说明；`final_base_phd.csv` 有 198 行整理地震目录，含 origin time、ML、MW 和定位误差字段。压裂侧数据包括 2,783 行 `20240314_VM_fracture_wells.csv` 和 4,098 行 `20240413_NHB_no_conv.csv`，字段覆盖井位、压裂段数、砂量、水量、CO2、压裂起止日期、盆地、区块、play/formation 等，可用于地震与水力压裂活动的时空关联研究。
desc_en: This Zenodo/GitHub package supports induced-seismicity analysis for the Neuquén Basin / Vaca Muerta shale play in Argentina, combining earthquake catalogs with hydraulic-fracturing and unconventional-well data. Local inspection of the GitHub `data/` directory confirmed `20240314_VM_quakes_all_soruces.csv` with 371 earthquake rows and fields for source, local date/time, latitude, longitude, depth, ML, location/depth error and intensity notes; `final_base_phd.csv` contains 198 curated event rows with origin time, ML, MW and location-error fields. The stimulation side includes 2,783 rows in `20240314_VM_fracture_wells.csv` and 4,098 rows in `20240413_NHB_no_conv.csv`, with fields for well coordinates, fracture stages, injected sand/water/CO2, fracture start/end dates, basin, concession area, play and formation.
openness: Zenodo release marked open under CC BY 4.0; GitHub repository openly accessible
evidence: Zenodo API for record 14065345 reports DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14065345, CC BY 4.0, and one archive file `RyanJamesSchultz/VM-ID-earthquake.zip` of 15,831,951 bytes. GitHub API for RyanJamesSchultz/VM-ID confirmed repository files and a `data/` directory. Local raw CSV inspection confirmed `20240314_VM_quakes_all_soruces.csv` with 371 rows x 10 columns, `20240314_VM_fracture_wells.csv` with 2,783 rows x 21 columns, `20240413_NHB_no_conv.csv` with 4,098 rows x 26 columns and `final_base_phd.csv` with 198 rows x 17 columns. Repository data directory also includes `CAT.mat`, `INJ.mat`, `INJ1.mat`, `INJ2.mat` and large official well table CSV inputs.
scale: basin-scale hydraulic-fracturing induced-seismicity catalog and stimulation-well dataset
feature_count: 371 raw earthquake rows; 198 curated event rows; 2,783 hydraulic-fracturing well rows; 4,098 unconventional-well rows inspected from raw CSV files
file_size: Zenodo archive 15,831,951 bytes; inspected GitHub raw files include 30,771-byte earthquake CSV, 510,340-byte fracture-well CSV, 898,687-byte unconventional-well CSV and 21,906-byte curated catalog CSV

name: Illinois Basin Decatur Project CO2-injection induced microseismicity catalog and seismic data
provider: CO2DataShare / NIRD Research Data Archive / Illinois State Geological Survey / Midwest Geological Sequestration Consortium
theme_canonical: CO2 sequestration induced microseismicity event catalog and seismic monitoring dataset
theme_raw: Illinois Basin - Decatur Project induced microseismicity event catalog and seismic data
coverage: Archer Daniels Midland / Illinois Basin - Decatur Project site, Decatur, Illinois, USA
license: Illinois Basin - DECATUR Project CO2 Injection Dataset license; NIRD access rights public
formats: CO2DataShare CKAN resources, ZIP resource packages, seismic event catalog spreadsheets/text, passive seismic event monitoring data, 3D seismic and VSP data, NIRD dataset metadata
access: Public NIRD dataset landing page and CO2DataShare portal/resource pages; resource downloads are mediated by the CO2DataShare/NIRD portal rather than simple unauthenticated command-line ZIP URLs
doi_or_citation: SINTEF (2022). Illinois Basin - Decatur Project Dataset. NIRD Research Data Archive. https://doi.org/10.11582/2022.00017; Greenberg, S. (2021). Illinois State Geological Survey (ISGS), Illinois Basin - Decatur Project (IBDP) Seismic Data, July 7, 2021. OSTI https://www.osti.gov/biblio/1854142
time_span: IBDP dataset temporal extent 2007-01-01 to 2020-12-01; induced microseismicity event catalog covers events after CO2 injection started on 2011-11-17 through 2018-07-02
notes: Accepted as a canonical CCS / CO2 sequestration artificial-seismicity dataset. CO2DataShare exposes the specific Seismic Event Catalog resource and the broader Seismic data resource. The direct CKAN download URL for the ZIP returned HTTP 400 to command-line access in v038, so the row does not claim simple direct ZIP download; instead it records the verified public landing pages, CKAN API metadata and portal-mediated public access.
desc_zh: 该 CO2DataShare / NIRD 数据集是 Illinois Basin - Decatur Project 在 Decatur, Illinois 的 CO2 地质封存监测数据，包含 CO2 注入诱发微地震事件目录和更大范围的地震监测数据。CO2DataShare API 明确列出 `Seismic Event Catalog` 资源，描述其为 IBDP induced microseismicity event catalog，覆盖 2011 年 11 月 17 日注入开始后至 2018 年 7 月 2 日的事件，并包含 all events、located events、source parameters、传感器信息、相对 CCS1 方位和 double-difference 重定位子集。更大的 `Seismic data` 资源包含高分辨率 3D seismic survey、time-lapse VSP 和 passive seismic events monitoring；NIRD 页面标注完整 IBDP 数据集为 public、67.5 GB，并给出 DOI 10.11582/2022.00017。
desc_en: This CO2DataShare / NIRD dataset is the Illinois Basin - Decatur Project monitoring data package for CO2 storage at Decatur, Illinois, including a CO2-injection induced microseismicity event catalog and broader seismic monitoring data. The CO2DataShare API lists a `Seismic Event Catalog` resource described as the IBDP induced microseismicity event catalog, covering events recorded after injection began on 2011-11-17 through 2018-07-02, with all events, located events, source parameters, sensor information, azimuth relative to CCS1 and a double-difference relocated subset. The larger `Seismic data` resource contains a high-resolution 3D seismic survey, time-lapse VSP and passive seismic events monitoring; the NIRD page marks the full IBDP dataset as public, 67.5 GB, with DOI 10.11582/2022.00017.
openness: public NIRD dataset with CO2DataShare public portal metadata; custom IBDP CO2 Injection Dataset license
source_type: CO2DataShare/NIRD CCS project induced microseismicity event catalog and seismic monitoring dataset
evidence: NIRD page for DOI 10.11582/2022.00017 identifies the Illinois Basin - Decatur Project Dataset as a 67.5 GB public dataset, active status, temporal extent 2007-01-01 to 2020-12-01, and states that it should be accessed from CO2DataShare. CO2DataShare CKAN API package_show for `illinois-basin-decatur-project-dataset` returned eight resources. The `Seismic Event Catalog` resource has size 9,507,198 bytes and describes the induced microseismicity event catalog, events after injection start on 2011-11-17 through 2018-07-02, all/located event spreadsheets, `.rdv` source-parameter text and a double-difference relocated subset. The `Seismic data` resource has size 15,437,891,729 bytes and contains 3D seismic survey, time-lapse VSP and passive seismic events monitoring.
scale: project-scale CO2 sequestration induced microseismicity catalog and seismic monitoring dataset
feature_count: CO2DataShare package exposes eight resources; seismic event catalog resource plus 15.44 GB seismic data resource; event count not exposed in inspected metadata
file_size: NIRD full dataset 67.5 GB; CO2DataShare Seismic Event Catalog 9,507,198 bytes; Seismic data 15,437,891,729 bytes

name: Aquistore CO2-injection microseismic and structural geology dataset
provider: University of Strathclyde / Aquistore / UKCCSRC project
theme_canonical: CO2 injection microseismic waveform and structural geology dataset
theme_raw: Microseismic data from Aquistore (Canada)
coverage: Aquistore CO2 storage site near Estevan, Saskatchewan, Canada
formats: PASCAL or ASCII microseismic data, ZIP seismic data archives, README RTF, field report DOCX, KMZ array file, Pure dataset landing page
access: University of Strathclyde Pure dataset landing page lists downloadable files; individual file endpoints may reject command-line requests with HTTP 403 but are exposed through the dataset page
doi_or_citation: Pytharouli, S., Lunn, R., Shipton, Z., Yfantis, G. and Kremer, Y. (2016). Microseismic data from Aquistore (Canada). University of Strathclyde. https://doi.org/10.15129/0536c9fa-38bd-4d8e-bfdd-637d9ee753e7
notes: Accepted as an open CCS / CO2 injection microseismic dataset. The Pure landing page states that the data cover a period when CO2 was being injected into the Aquistore injection well at 3.5 km depth, and that the project aimed to identify microseismic events potentially related to CO2 injection. v039 lychee verification uses the dataset landing page and CC BY license page; direct file endpoints for README/DOCX/ZIP returned HTTP 403 to command-line access, so access is described as landing-page mediated rather than simple direct-download URLs.
desc_zh: 该 University of Strathclyde 数据集发布加拿大 Aquistore CO2 封存场地 2015 年 4 月 26 日至 6 月 21 日的微地震与构造地质数据，覆盖 CO2 正在 3.5 km 深注入井中注入的时期。页面说明数据由距注入井约 150 m、埋深约 50 cm 的短周期微地震阵列连续采集，阵列包括 1 个三分量传感器和 3 个单分量传感器，采样率 1000 Hz，目标是识别可能与 CO2 注入相关的微地震事件并用于成像深部潜在流动通道。数据页列出 README、现场报告、KMZ 阵列文件以及 16 个 Seismic_Data ZIP 包，ZIP 总量约 55.18 GB，许可证均标注 CC BY 4.0。
desc_en: This University of Strathclyde dataset releases microseismic and structural geological data from the Aquistore CO2 storage site in Canada for 2015-04-26 to 2015-06-21, a period when CO2 was being injected into the Aquistore injection well at 3.5 km depth. The landing page states that continuous data were collected at 1000 Hz by a short-period microseismic array about 150 m from the injection well and buried at about 50 cm depth, consisting of one three-component and three one-component sensors, to identify possible CO2-injection-related microseismic events and image potential deep flow pathways. The page lists README, field report, KMZ array file and 16 Seismic_Data ZIP packages totaling about 55.18 GB, all marked CC BY 4.0.
openness: dataset landing page lists files under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: university repository CCS microseismic waveform and structural geology dataset
evidence: University of Strathclyde Pure page describes microseismic and structural geological data collected at Aquistore during CO2 injection from 2015-04-26 to 2015-06-21, with 1000 Hz continuous acquisition by a short-period array. The page lists DOI 10.15129/0536c9fa-38bd-4d8e-bfdd-637d9ee753e7, publisher University of Strathclyde, geographical coverage Estevan, Saskatchewan, and file-level CC BY 4.0 licensing. Listed files include README.rtf 4.24 KB, Canada_Field_Report.docx 7.29 MB, Strath_Array.kmz 856 bytes, and 16 Seismic_Data ZIP files ranging from 2.6 GB to 4.14 GB with summed listed size about 55.18 GB. Local lychee verification confirmed the dataset landing page and CC BY 4.0 license page; direct file endpoints returned HTTP 403 to command-line requests.
scale: site-scale CO2 injection microseismic waveform and structural geology dataset
feature_count: continuous 1000 Hz microseismic array data from one three-component and three one-component sensors; 16 listed Seismic_Data ZIP packages plus README, field report and KMZ array file
file_size: 16 Seismic_Data ZIP files sum to about 55.18 GB by listed sizes; README 4.24 KB; field report 7.29 MB; KMZ 856 bytes

name: CO2CRC Otway Stage 3 injection-induced microseismic SEGY seismogram dataset
provider: Zenodo / CO2CRC Otway Project Stage 3 researchers
theme_canonical: CO2 storage injection-induced microseismic SEGY waveform dataset
theme_raw: Injection-induced microseismic events CO2CRC Otway Project Stage 3
coverage: CO2CRC Otway Project Stage 3, Otway International Test Centre, Victoria, Australia
formats: SEGY raw seismogram ZIP, TXT data description and event catalog, trace-header outline file, Zenodo landing page
access: Zenodo landing page with direct API content URLs for RAW_seismograms.zip, DATA_DESCRIPTION.txt and SEGY trace-header outline file
doi_or_citation: Zenodo record: Injection-induced microseismic events CO2CRC Otway Project Stage 3. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7574468
time_span: event catalog rows in DATA_DESCRIPTION span 2020-10-29 to 2021-11-06
notes: Accepted as an open CCS / CO2 storage injection-induced microseismic waveform dataset. The Zenodo description states that the repository contains seismograms of fluid-induced microseismic events during CO2CRC Otway Project Stage 3, recorded by enhanced distributed acoustic sensing systems in five approximately 1,600 m deep wells. The raw waveform package is large but directly exposed through Zenodo; local inspection was limited to the small description and trace-header files plus URL verification.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集发布 CO2CRC Otway Project Stage 3 期间流体注入诱发微地震事件的原始 SEGY seismograms，是 CO2 储存现场的开放微地震波形数据。页面说明数据由五口约 1,600 m 深井中的 enhanced distributed acoustic sensing 系统记录，SEGY 格式可被常见地震处理软件读取。本地检查 `DATA_DESCRIPTION.txt` 确认其中列出事件目录，字段包括 EVENT_ID、UTC 时间、UTM East/North、深度、震级、corner frequency 以及 Strike/Dip/Rake；可解析出 17 个事件行，时间从 2020-10-29 到 2021-11-06。`RAW_seismograms.zip` 大小约 734 MB，提供事件波形，另有 trace-header outline 文件辅助读取 SEGY 头信息。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset releases raw SEGY seismograms for fluid-injection induced microseismic events during CO2CRC Otway Project Stage 3, making it an open waveform source for CO2 storage microseismic monitoring. The record states that the data were recorded by enhanced distributed acoustic sensing systems in five approximately 1,600 m deep wells and that the SEGY files should be readable by standard seismic-processing software. Local inspection of `DATA_DESCRIPTION.txt` confirmed an event catalog with EVENT_ID, UTC time, UTM East/North, depth, magnitude, corner frequency and strike/dip/rake fields; 17 event rows were parsed, spanning 2020-10-29 to 2021-11-06. `RAW_seismograms.zip` is about 734 MB and provides the event waveforms, with an additional trace-header outline file for SEGY interpretation.
openness: Zenodo dataset under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0
source_type: Zenodo CCS project injection-induced microseismic waveform dataset
evidence: Zenodo API for record 7574468 reports resource_type Dataset, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.7574468, CC BY 4.0 license and three files: RAW_seismograms.zip 734,125,423 bytes, DATA_DESCRIPTION.txt 2,056 bytes and SEGY_trace_headers_outline.csv 14,139 bytes. The Zenodo description states that the repository contains seismograms of fluid-induced microseismic events during CO2CRC Otway Project Stage 3, recorded by enhanced distributed acoustic sensing systems in five ~1,600 m deep wells. Local inspection of DATA_DESCRIPTION.txt confirmed file naming format `event_EVENT_ID_WELL_ID_strain_geom.sgy`, well IDs CRC-3 to CRC-7, UTM zone 54H, depth below mean sea level, and 17 event rows with fields for time, UTM coordinates, depth, magnitude, corner frequency and mechanism.
scale: site-scale CO2 storage injection-induced microseismic DAS/SEGY waveform dataset
feature_count: 17 event rows parsed from DATA_DESCRIPTION.txt; raw SEGY seismograms recorded in five ~1,600 m deep wells
file_size: RAW_seismograms.zip 734,125,423 bytes; DATA_DESCRIPTION.txt 2,056 bytes; SEGY trace-header outline file 14,139 bytes per Zenodo API

name: Korean Peninsula earthquake-explosion seismoacoustic fusion waveform dataset
provider: Zenodo / Miro Ronac Giannone et al. / KIGAM / KMA / Han et al.
theme_canonical: Earthquake-explosion seismic and infrasound waveform discrimination dataset
theme_raw: Deep Multimodal Learning for Seismoacoustic Fusion; KIGAM explosion database; KMA and Han et al. earthquake events
coverage: Korean Peninsula; seismic and infrasound waveforms/features for BRD, CHN and KSG arrays/stations
formats: Zenodo ZIP packages containing seismic waveform windows, infrasound waveform windows and Cardinal feature files; GitHub notebooks, spreadsheets and array location ZIPs
access: Zenodo open dataset record with direct file downloads; companion GitHub repository documents extraction, preprocessing and model workflow
doi_or_citation: Concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.10795252; version DOI 10.5281/zenodo.10810712; Ronac Giannone et al. manuscript/repository: Deep Multimodal Learning for Seismoacoustic Fusion to Improve Earthquake-Explosion Discrimination within the Korean Peninsula
time_span: Event windows from KIGAM explosion database and KMA/Han et al. (2023) earthquake catalogs; Zenodo publication year 2024
notes: Accepted in v041 as a distinct artificial-earthquake discrimination dataset, not a duplicate of GTUNE, WFNE or DAG-4. The dataset is derived/windowed for machine-learning use: KIGAM supplied explosion origin times and locations, while earthquakes come from KMA and Han et al. (2023). It is especially valuable because it pairs seismic and infrasound channels for the same earthquake/explosion discrimination task over the Korean Peninsula.
seen_in_passes: v041 korean peninsula seismoacoustic explosion-discrimination pass
desc_zh: 该数据集面向韩国半岛地震-爆炸判别，开放发布了由 KIGAM 爆炸数据库、KMA 地震目录和 Han 等（2023）地震事件构建的地震与次声波形窗口。Zenodo 版本记录包含 27 个 ZIP 文件，总量约 10.46 GiB，覆盖 BRD、CHN、KSG 三组台阵/台站的地震波形、次声波形和 Cardinal 特征文件。它不是普通自然地震目录，而是明确服务于人工爆炸与地震识别的多模态开放训练数据。
desc_en: This dataset supports earthquake-explosion discrimination in the Korean Peninsula by publishing windowed seismic and infrasound data derived from the KIGAM explosion database, KMA earthquake events and Han et al. (2023) earthquake events. The Zenodo version record exposes 27 ZIP packages totaling about 10.46 GiB, including waveform and Cardinal feature files for BRD, CHN and KSG arrays/stations. It is a machine-learning oriented seismoacoustic benchmark for artificial explosions versus earthquakes rather than a generic natural-earthquake catalog.
source_type: repository DOI dataset and companion GitHub workflow
evidence: Zenodo API record 10810712 identifies the resource as a Dataset, access_right open, license cc-by-4.0, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.10810712 and concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.10795252. Its description states that it contains earthquake-explosion seismic and infrasound data, with KIGAM providing explosion origin times/locations and KMA/Han et al. providing earthquake events. The API lists 27 downloadable ZIP files totaling 11,230,842,321 bytes (10.46 GiB), including 9 explosion-related KIGAM packages and 18 earthquake packages. GitHub README states the repository documents extraction and processing of seismic/infrasound data for 'Deep Multimodal Learning for Seismoacoustic Fusion to Improve Earthquake-Explosion Discrimination within the Korean Peninsula' and points to the Zenodo concept DOI.
scale: regional seismoacoustic benchmark dataset; Korean Peninsula; multi-station waveform windows and features
